tasks.title: Codebase Refactoring and Cleanup
tasks.description: Resolve merge conflicts, remove dead code/stubs, implement centralized logging, run depcheck, clean directories, and organize AI configs
tasks.details: 1. Resolve merge conflicts in profile/page.tsx, StatsOverview.tsx, EvolutionChart.tsx, PositionStats.tsx using git mergetool. 2. Remove 501 stub routes (tournament/create, discovery/insert-manual) or implement minimally. 3. Create src/lib/logger.ts with Winston: `npm i winston`, implement logger.error/replace console.error. 4. Run `npx depcheck --ignores='@types/*,eslint*'`, remove unused deps. 5. Remove material-symbols from package.json. 6. Add ./~/, coverage/, dist/ to .gitignore. 7. Consolidate AI configs to docs/ai-tools/, keep CLAUDE.md as SSOT. 8. Extract stateful logic from 7 admin tab panels to custom hooks (useState/useEffect). 9. Add npm script 'gen:types': 'supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/database.ts'. 10. Run naming audit with ESLint naming rules.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build`, `npm test`, verify no console.error in browser devtools, check bundle size reduction, validate types with `tsc --noEmit`, test logger with unit test: expect(logger.error('test')).toBeCalled()

subtasks.title: Resolve Merge Conflicts in Profile and Stats Components
subtasks.description: Resolve all outstanding git merge conflicts in profile/page.tsx, StatsOverview.tsx, EvolutionChart.tsx, and PositionStats.tsx using git mergetool or manual resolution.
subtasks.details: Run `git status` to confirm all conflicted files. Use `git mergetool` or manually open each file and resolve conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) for: (1) src/app/profile/page.tsx, (2) components/StatsOverview.tsx, (3) components/EvolutionChart.tsx, (4) components/PositionStats.tsx. After resolving each file, run `git add <file>` to stage. Ensure no conflict markers remain by running `grep -r '<<<<<<' src/`. Verify the resolved code compiles and renders correctly. Commit the resolution with a descriptive message like 'fix: resolve merge conflicts in profile and stats components'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `grep -r '<<<<<<' src/` to confirm zero conflict markers. Run `npm run build` to verify all resolved files compile without errors. Manually review each resolved file to ensure correct logic was preserved from both branches.

subtasks.title: Remove or Minimally Implement 501 Stub Routes
subtasks.description: Evaluate and either remove or provide minimal implementations for the 501 stub routes: tournament/create and discovery/insert-manual.
subtasks.details: Locate the stub route handlers for `tournament/create` and `discovery/insert-manual` in the Next.js app router (likely under src/app/api/ or src/app/(routes)/). For each route: (1) Assess if the route is referenced anywhere in the codebase using `grep -r 'tournament/create' src/` and `grep -r 'discovery/insert-manual' src/`. (2) If unreferenced, delete the route file entirely. (3) If referenced, implement a minimal version that returns a proper HTTP 501 with a JSON body `{ error: 'Not implemented', route: '<name>' }` or a basic working stub with a TODO comment. Remove any dead UI components that exclusively link to these routes. Update any navigation or link references accordingly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build` to confirm no broken imports. Use `curl` or a REST client to hit each route endpoint and verify either a 404 (removed) or a proper 501 JSON response (stubbed). Run `grep -r 'tournament/create\|discovery/insert-manual' src/` to confirm all references are handled.

subtasks.title: Set Up Winston Centralized Logger and Replace console.error
subtasks.description: Install Winston, create src/lib/logger.ts with a configured logger instance, and replace all console.error calls throughout the codebase with the centralized logger.
subtasks.details: Run `npm i winston` and `npm i -D @types/winston` if needed. Create `src/lib/logger.ts` exporting a Winston logger configured with: (1) Console transport for development (colorized, timestamp), (2) File transport for production (error.log, combined.log), (3) Log levels: error, warn, info, debug. Export named functions: `logger.error()`, `logger.warn()`, `logger.info()`, `logger.debug()`. Run `grep -rn 'console\.error' src/` to find all occurrences. Replace each `console.error(...)` with `logger.error(...)`, importing from `@/lib/logger`. Also replace `console.warn` and `console.log` in non-UI code where appropriate. Ensure logger is not used in client components directly — wrap with a server-safe guard or use a separate client logger utility if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write a unit test: import logger, spy on the transport, call `logger.error('test')`, and assert it was called. Run `grep -rn 'console\.error' src/` to confirm zero remaining occurrences. Run `npm run build` and `npm test` to validate no regressions. Check browser devtools for absence of console.error output.

subtasks.title: Run Dependency Audit and Remove Unused Dependencies
subtasks.description: Run depcheck to identify unused dependencies, remove them from package.json, and specifically remove the material-symbols package.
subtasks.details: Run `npx depcheck --ignores='@types/*,eslint*'` and capture the output listing unused dependencies. For each flagged unused dependency: (1) Verify it is truly unused by searching the codebase (`grep -r '<package-name>' src/`). (2) Remove confirmed unused packages with `npm uninstall <package>`. Specifically run `npm uninstall material-symbols` as it is confirmed unused. After removals, run `npm install` to update package-lock.json. Review package.json before and after to confirm removals. Check for any peer dependency warnings. Document removed packages in a comment or commit message.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build` after removals to confirm no missing module errors. Run `npx depcheck` again to verify the unused list is empty or reduced. Check bundle size with `npm run build -- --analyze` or similar to confirm size reduction.

subtasks.title: Update .gitignore with Missing Directory Patterns
subtasks.description: Add ./~/, coverage/, and dist/ directory patterns to .gitignore to prevent accidental commits of generated and temporary files.
subtasks.details: Open the root `.gitignore` file. Add the following entries if not already present: `./~/` (tilde/temp directories), `coverage/` (test coverage output), `dist/` (build distribution output). Also verify that `.next/`, `node_modules/`, and other common Next.js ignores are present. If any of these directories currently exist in the git index (tracked), run `git rm -r --cached coverage/ dist/` to untrack them. Commit the .gitignore update. Optionally add `*.log` and `error.log` patterns to complement the Winston logger setup from subtask 3.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `git check-ignore -v coverage/test.txt dist/bundle.js` to confirm the patterns are active. Create a dummy file in each directory and verify `git status` does not show them as untracked. Run `npm run build` to confirm .gitignore changes don't affect the build process.

subtasks.title: Consolidate AI Configuration Files to docs/ai-tools/
subtasks.description: Move and consolidate all AI tool configuration files into docs/ai-tools/, establishing CLAUDE.md as the Single Source of Truth (SSOT) for AI assistant context.
subtasks.details: Audit the repository for all AI-related config files (e.g., .cursorrules, .aider.conf, copilot instructions, any existing CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) using `find . -name '*.md' | xargs grep -l 'AI\|assistant\|claude\|cursor' 2>/dev/null`. Create the `docs/ai-tools/` directory. Move all relevant AI config files into `docs/ai-tools/`. Designate `CLAUDE.md` (at root or in docs/ai-tools/) as the SSOT — it should contain: project overview, tech stack, coding conventions, file structure, and key decisions. Other AI tool configs should reference or import from CLAUDE.md where possible. Update any README references to AI config files. Add a note in CLAUDE.md indicating it is the SSOT.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify `docs/ai-tools/` directory exists and contains all AI config files. Confirm CLAUDE.md has a clear SSOT header comment. Run `git status` to ensure no AI config files remain in unexpected locations. Run `npm run build` to confirm no build-time references to moved files are broken.

subtasks.title: Extract Stateful Logic from Admin Tab Panels into Custom Hooks
subtasks.description: Refactor all 7 admin tab panel components by extracting their useState and useEffect logic into dedicated custom hooks for better separation of concerns.
subtasks.details: Identify all 7 admin tab panel components (likely under src/app/admin/ or src/components/admin/). For each panel: (1) Audit all useState and useEffect calls within the component. (2) Create a corresponding custom hook file, e.g., `usePlayersTabPanel.ts`, `useTournamentsTabPanel.ts`, etc., in a `src/hooks/admin/` directory. (3) Move all stateful logic (state declarations, effects, derived values, handlers) into the hook. (4) Return the necessary state and handlers from the hook. (5) Update the panel component to call the hook and destructure its return values. Ensure hooks follow the Rules of Hooks. Add TypeScript types for hook return values.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build` and `tsc --noEmit` to confirm no TypeScript errors. Manually test each admin tab panel in the browser to verify functionality is unchanged. Write unit tests for at least 2 hooks using React Testing Library's `renderHook`, asserting initial state and state transitions.

subtasks.title: Add Supabase Type Generation npm Script
subtasks.description: Add a 'gen:types' npm script to package.json that generates TypeScript types from the local Supabase schema into src/types/database.ts.
subtasks.details: Open `package.json` and add to the `scripts` section: `"gen:types": "supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/database.ts"`. Ensure `supabase` CLI is listed as a dev dependency or is available globally. Create the `src/types/` directory if it doesn't exist. Run `npm run gen:types` once to generate the initial `database.ts` file. Verify the generated file contains proper TypeScript interfaces/types for all Supabase tables. Add `src/types/database.ts` to `.gitignore` if it should be generated at build time, OR commit the initial version if it should be versioned. Update any existing manual type definitions that duplicate the generated types to import from `src/types/database.ts` instead.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run gen:types` and verify `src/types/database.ts` is created/updated with valid TypeScript. Run `tsc --noEmit` to confirm the generated types are valid. Check that at least one existing component can import and use a type from `src/types/database.ts` without errors.

subtasks.title: Run ESLint Naming Convention Audit and Fix Violations
subtasks.description: Configure ESLint naming convention rules and run a full audit across the codebase, fixing all naming violations for variables, functions, components, and files.
subtasks.details: Add or update ESLint configuration (`.eslintrc.js` or `eslint.config.js`) with `@typescript-eslint/naming-convention` rules enforcing: (1) camelCase for variables and functions, (2) PascalCase for React components, classes, and type/interface names, (3) UPPER_CASE for constants, (4) camelCase for file names (or kebab-case per project convention). Run `npx eslint src/ --rule '@typescript-eslint/naming-convention: error' --fix` to auto-fix where possible. Manually fix remaining violations reported by ESLint. Pay special attention to the 7 admin hooks created in subtask 7. Document any intentional exceptions with `// eslint-disable-next-line` comments with justification.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npx eslint src/ --max-warnings 0` and confirm zero naming-related warnings or errors. Run `npm run build` to confirm no regressions from renames. Spot-check 5-10 renamed identifiers in the browser to confirm runtime behavior is unchanged.

subtasks.title: Full Build, Test, and Validation Pass
subtasks.description: Run a comprehensive build, test, and validation suite across all refactoring changes to confirm the codebase is stable, type-safe, and meets all success criteria.
subtasks.details: Execute the following validation steps in order: (1) `tsc --noEmit` — confirm zero TypeScript errors across the entire codebase. (2) `npm run build` — confirm production build succeeds with no errors or warnings. (3) `npm test` — run the full test suite and confirm all tests pass. (4) `npx eslint src/ --max-warnings 0` — confirm zero ESLint violations. (5) `grep -r '<<<<<<' src/` — confirm zero merge conflict markers. (6) `grep -rn 'console\.error' src/` — confirm zero raw console.error calls. (7) `npx depcheck --ignores='@types/*,eslint*'` — confirm no unused dependencies remain. (8) Open the app in a browser and check DevTools console for zero errors. (9) Compare bundle size before/after using build output stats. Document all results in a validation report or PR description.
subtasks.testStrategy: All 8 validation commands must exit with code 0. Bundle size must be equal to or smaller than pre-refactoring baseline. Zero console.error entries in browser DevTools during a full app walkthrough covering profile page, stats components, and all 7 admin tab panels.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down this refactoring task into discrete, testable subtasks: (1) Merge conflict resolution per file, (2) Stub route removal/implementation strategy, (3) Winston logger setup and integration, (4) Dependency audit and removal, (5) .gitignore updates, (6) AI config consolidation, (7) Admin hook extraction per panel, (8) Type generation script setup, (9) ESLint naming audit, (10) Build and test validation. For each, define success criteria and testing approach.

tasks.title: Project Documentation Setup
tasks.description: Create CONTRIBUTING.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, API.md, DATABASE.md, GitHub templates, labels, milestone, and update README.md
tasks.details: 1. Create docs/ folder structure. 2. docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: Git workflow (branch-per-task, PR checklist), dev setup, testing reqs. 3. docs/DEVELOPMENT.md: Supabase local start, env vars, migrations, seed. 4. docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: Mermaid diagram of app flow, Supabase schema. 5. docs/API.md: Table of 40+ routes (method/path/params/response). 6. docs/DATABASE.md: Schema doc, ERD Mermaid, views list. 7. .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md + feature_request.md. 8. .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with Phase 2 checklist. 9. GitHub labels: bug,feature,refactor,docs,redesign,phase-2. 10. Milestone 'Phase 2: Redesign'. 11. Update README.md with badges, quickstart, deploy links.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate all docs render correctly in GitHub/Markdown preview, check ISSUE/PR templates appear on GitHub, verify milestone creation, run `npm run build` passes

subtasks.title: Create docs/ Folder Structure
subtasks.description: Set up the top-level docs/ directory and all required subdirectories to house project documentation files.
subtasks.details: Create the docs/ folder at the project root. Add placeholder README or .gitkeep files in each subdirectory if needed to ensure the structure is committed. Confirm the folder layout matches: docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/DEVELOPMENT.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/API.md, docs/DATABASE.md. Also ensure .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ directory exists for templates. Document the folder structure in a brief index comment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify folder structure exists in the repository with correct paths. Run a directory tree check to confirm all expected paths are present.

subtasks.title: Create docs/CONTRIBUTING.md with Git Workflow
subtasks.description: Write the CONTRIBUTING.md document covering Git workflow conventions, branch-per-task strategy, PR checklist, dev setup instructions, and testing requirements.
subtasks.details: Include sections: (1) Git Workflow — branch naming convention (e.g., task/27-docs-setup), commit message format, rebase vs merge policy; (2) Pull Request Checklist — items like tests passing, lint clean, docs updated; (3) Development Setup — prerequisites, install steps, env var setup; (4) Testing Requirements — unit, integration, and e2e test expectations. Use clear headings and code blocks for commands. Target audience: new contributors.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render the file in GitHub Markdown preview and verify all sections display correctly, links resolve, and code blocks are properly formatted.

subtasks.title: Create docs/DEVELOPMENT.md with Local Setup Guide
subtasks.description: Write the DEVELOPMENT.md document detailing how to run the project locally using Supabase, including environment variables, migrations, and seed data.
subtasks.details: Include sections: (1) Prerequisites — Node version, Supabase CLI install; (2) Supabase Local Start — `supabase start` command, expected output; (3) Environment Variables — list all required .env variables with descriptions and example values; (4) Running Migrations — `supabase db push` or migration commands; (5) Seeding Data — seed script commands and expected outcomes; (6) Starting the Dev Server — `npm run dev` and any additional steps. Include troubleshooting tips for common issues.
subtasks.testStrategy: Follow the guide from scratch on a clean environment to verify all steps work end-to-end. Confirm `npm run build` passes after setup.

subtasks.title: Create docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with Mermaid Diagrams
subtasks.description: Write the ARCHITECTURE.md document including a Mermaid diagram of the application flow and a Mermaid diagram of the Supabase schema.
subtasks.details: Include sections: (1) Overview — high-level description of the system architecture (frontend, backend, database, auth); (2) Application Flow Diagram — Mermaid flowchart showing user interactions, API calls, Supabase RLS, and data flow; (3) Supabase Schema Diagram — Mermaid ER diagram showing tables, relationships, and key fields; (4) Key Design Decisions — brief rationale for major architectural choices. Ensure Mermaid syntax is valid and renders on GitHub.
subtasks.testStrategy: Paste Mermaid code blocks into the Mermaid Live Editor (mermaid.live) to validate syntax. Render the file on GitHub to confirm diagrams display correctly.

subtasks.title: Create docs/API.md with Full Route Documentation
subtasks.description: Write the API.md document containing a comprehensive table of all 40+ API routes with method, path, parameters, and response details.
subtasks.details: Structure the document with grouped sections by resource (e.g., Auth, Users, Teams, Tournaments, Brackets). For each route, provide a Markdown table row with: HTTP Method, Path, Query/Body Params, Auth Required (yes/no), Success Response shape, and Error Codes. Include at least 40 routes. Add a brief intro section explaining base URL, authentication headers, and response envelope format. Use collapsible sections or anchored headings for navigation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cross-reference routes against the actual codebase route handlers to verify completeness and accuracy. Render on GitHub to confirm table formatting is correct.

subtasks.title: Create docs/DATABASE.md with Schema Documentation
subtasks.description: Write the DATABASE.md document covering the full database schema, an ERD Mermaid diagram, and a list of database views.
subtasks.details: Include sections: (1) Schema Overview — list all tables with column names, types, constraints, and descriptions; (2) ERD Diagram — Mermaid erDiagram block showing all tables and their relationships (PK, FK, cardinality); (3) Views List — table listing all database views with their purpose and the underlying query summary; (4) Indexes and Policies — document key indexes and RLS policies. Ensure accuracy by referencing the actual Supabase migration files.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate Mermaid ERD syntax in Mermaid Live Editor. Cross-reference schema documentation against migration files for accuracy. Render on GitHub to confirm formatting.

subtasks.title: Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md
subtasks.description: Create the GitHub bug report issue template with structured fields to capture reproducible bug information from contributors.
subtasks.details: Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md with YAML front matter (name, about, title prefix, labels: bug, assignees). Include template sections: (1) Bug Description; (2) Steps to Reproduce (numbered list); (3) Expected Behavior; (4) Actual Behavior; (5) Screenshots/Logs (optional); (6) Environment (OS, browser, Node version, app version); (7) Additional Context. Use GitHub issue template syntax with HTML comments as guidance text for contributors.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the repository's 'New Issue' page on GitHub and verify the bug report template appears as a selectable option with correct fields and labels pre-applied.

subtasks.title: Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md
subtasks.description: Create the GitHub feature request issue template with structured fields to capture feature proposals from contributors.
subtasks.details: Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md with YAML front matter (name, about, title prefix, labels: feature, assignees). Include template sections: (1) Feature Summary; (2) Problem / Motivation — what problem does this solve?; (3) Proposed Solution; (4) Alternatives Considered; (5) Acceptance Criteria (checklist format); (6) Additional Context / Mockups. Use HTML comments as guidance text. Ensure the label 'feature' matches the label created in subtask 10.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the repository's 'New Issue' page on GitHub and verify the feature request template appears as a selectable option with correct fields and the 'feature' label pre-applied.

subtasks.title: Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with Phase 2 Checklist
subtasks.description: Create the GitHub pull request template with a comprehensive Phase 2 checklist to standardize PR submissions.
subtasks.details: Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md. Include sections: (1) Description — summary of changes and motivation; (2) Related Issue(s) — 'Closes #' links; (3) Type of Change — checkboxes (bug fix, feature, refactor, docs, redesign); (4) Phase 2 Checklist — checkboxes covering: tests added/updated, lint passes, build passes (`npm run build`), Tailwind tokens used (no inline styles), components added to gallery, docs updated, screenshots attached for UI changes, reviewed by at least one peer; (5) Additional Notes. Use GitHub Markdown checkbox syntax `- [ ]`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Open a new pull request on GitHub and verify the template auto-populates in the PR description field with all checklist items visible and interactive.

subtasks.title: Create GitHub Labels and Phase 2 Milestone
subtasks.description: Create all required GitHub labels (bug, feature, refactor, docs, redesign, phase-2) and the 'Phase 2: Redesign' milestone via the GitHub UI or CLI.
subtasks.details: Using the GitHub CLI (`gh`) or the GitHub web UI, create the following labels with appropriate colors: bug (red #d73a4a), feature (green #0075ca), refactor (yellow #e4e669), docs (blue #0052cc), redesign (purple #7057ff), phase-2 (orange #f9d0c4). Then create a milestone titled 'Phase 2: Redesign' with a description summarizing the redesign goals and an appropriate due date. Document the creation commands or steps in a brief internal note.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the repository's Labels page and Milestones page on GitHub to confirm all 6 labels exist with correct colors and the milestone is created with the correct title and description.

subtasks.title: Update README.md with Badges, Quickstart, and Deploy Links
subtasks.description: Update the root README.md to include status badges, a quickstart guide, and deployment links to bring it up to project standards.
subtasks.details: Update README.md with: (1) Badges — build status, license, GitHub issues, milestone progress, tech stack badges (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind); (2) Project Overview — concise description and key features list; (3) Quickstart — condensed setup steps linking to docs/DEVELOPMENT.md for full details; (4) Architecture Overview — brief summary linking to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md; (5) Deploy Links — production URL, staging URL, Supabase dashboard link (if public); (6) Documentation Index — links to all docs/ files and .github templates; (7) Contributing — link to docs/CONTRIBUTING.md. Ensure all badge URLs are valid and resolve correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render README.md on GitHub repository root to verify all badges load correctly, links resolve to correct targets, and the quickstart section is accurate. Run `npm run build` to confirm no regressions.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose documentation creation into focused subtasks: (1) docs/ folder structure setup, (2) CONTRIBUTING.md with Git workflow, (3) DEVELOPMENT.md with local setup, (4) ARCHITECTURE.md with Mermaid diagrams, (5) API.md route documentation, (6) DATABASE.md schema documentation, (7) Bug report template, (8) Feature request template, (9) PR template with checklist, (10) GitHub labels and milestone creation, (11) README.md updates with badges and links. Assign ownership and validation criteria for each.

tasks.title: Visual Brainstorming and Design System Generation
tasks.description: Install UI/UX Pro Max skill, create design-refs/, generate MASTER.md with complete design system specs and page specs
tasks.details: 1. `npx skills add https://github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill --skill ui-ux-pro-max`. 2. Create design-refs/ with screenshots: VirtualNexusArena.com, 21st.dev, Dribbble esports dashboards. 3. Run UI/UX Pro Max skill targeting design-system/MASTER.md output. 4. MASTER.md sections: color palette (primary/secondary/accent/semantic), typography scale (fonts/weights/sizes), spacing scale (4px grid), component specs (button/card/modal/table/sidebar/navbar), anti-patterns. 5. Create design-system/pages/ with specs for profile.md, team.md, auth.md, etc. 6. Manual validation/adjustments. 7. Finalize primary color decision (blue/orange).
tasks.testStrategy: Validate MASTER.md completeness against checklist, screenshot specs match references, test color contrast with WAVE tool (WCAG AA), verify all pages have spec files

subtasks.title: Install and Configure UI/UX Pro Max Skill
subtasks.description: Install the UI/UX Pro Max skill via npx and verify it is correctly configured and ready to run against the project.
subtasks.details: Run `npx skills add https://github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill --skill ui-ux-pro-max` in the project root. Verify the skill is registered and accessible. Check for any undocumented configuration requirements (e.g., API keys, config files, output directory setup). Create the `design-system/` and `design-refs/` directories as scaffolding for subsequent steps. Document any installation issues or version pinning needed for reproducibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npx skills list` or equivalent to confirm `ui-ux-pro-max` appears. Verify `design-system/` and `design-refs/` directories exist. Confirm no errors in skill invocation with a dry-run or help flag.

subtasks.title: Collect and Curate Design Reference Screenshots
subtasks.description: Gather visual design references from VirtualNexusArena.com, 21st.dev, and Dribbble esports dashboards and store them in the design-refs/ directory.
subtasks.details: Capture full-page and component-level screenshots from: (1) VirtualNexusArena.com — focus on navigation, dashboard layout, color usage; (2) 21st.dev — focus on component design patterns, typography, spacing; (3) Dribbble esports dashboards — search for 'esports dashboard', 'gaming UI', 'team management UI' and capture 5–10 high-quality references. Organize into subdirectories: `design-refs/virtualnexusarena/`, `design-refs/21stdev/`, `design-refs/dribbble/`. Add a `design-refs/README.md` annotating key design decisions observed in each reference (color palette, layout patterns, component styles).
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all three source directories exist with at least 3 screenshots each. Confirm README.md annotations are present. Review references with a team member for design relevance and quality.

subtasks.title: Generate MASTER.md Color Palette and Base Design Tokens
subtasks.description: Run the UI/UX Pro Max skill to generate the initial MASTER.md and define the complete color palette section including primary, secondary, accent, and semantic colors.
subtasks.details: Run the UI/UX Pro Max skill targeting `design-system/MASTER.md` as output, using design-refs/ as input context. In the generated MASTER.md, ensure the color palette section includes: (1) Primary color candidates (blue #0d7ff2 vs orange — document both with hex/HSL values); (2) Secondary colors; (3) Accent colors; (4) Semantic colors (success, warning, error, info) with WCAG AA contrast ratios documented. Add CSS variable naming convention (e.g., `--primary`, `--surface-1`, `--surface-2`). Validate all colors with WAVE tool or contrast checker for WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components).
subtasks.testStrategy: Check MASTER.md exists and contains a complete color palette section. Validate all color pairs meet WCAG AA contrast ratios using the WAVE tool or browser devtools. Confirm both blue and orange primary options are documented for final decision.

subtasks.title: Define Typography and Spacing Scale in MASTER.md
subtasks.description: Document the complete typography scale (fonts, weights, sizes) and spacing scale (4px grid system) in MASTER.md.
subtasks.details: Add typography section to MASTER.md: (1) Font families — primary display font (Google Fonts candidate), body font, monospace font (Fira Code for data/stats); (2) Type scale — page-title, section-title, card-title, body-lg, body-md, body-sm, caption, label sizes in rem with px equivalents; (3) Font weights — regular (400), medium (500), semibold (600), bold (700); (4) Line heights and letter spacing per scale level. Add spacing section: (1) 4px base grid — define t-shirt sizes (xs=4px, sm=8px, md=16px, lg=24px, xl=32px, 2xl=48px, 3xl=64px); (2) Component-specific spacing guidelines; (3) Layout grid (12-column, gutters, margins). Reference Tailwind config extension mappings for each token.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify MASTER.md typography and spacing sections are complete. Cross-reference font choices against Google Fonts availability. Confirm spacing scale aligns with 4px grid (all values divisible by 4). Check Tailwind config extension mappings are documented.

subtasks.title: Write Component Specifications in MASTER.md
subtasks.description: Create detailed component specifications for button, card, modal, table, sidebar, and navbar components in MASTER.md.
subtasks.details: For each component, document: variants, states, sizing, spacing, color tokens used, and accessibility requirements. Components to specify: (1) Button — variants (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive), sizes (sm/md/lg), states (default, hover, active, disabled, loading), focus-visible ring style; (2) Card — variants (default, glass, elevated), padding, border-radius, shadow tokens, hover state (`--card-hover`); (3) Modal — overlay, container sizing, header/body/footer layout, close button, keyboard trap (Esc to close); (4) Table/DataTable — header, row, sort, filter, pagination, empty state, loading skeleton; (5) Sidebar — collapsed/expanded states, nav-item-active style, glass effect using CSS var tokens; (6) Navbar — logo placement, nav links, user avatar, mobile hamburger. Include code snippets showing token usage.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review each component spec against the design references collected in subtask 2. Verify all states are documented. Confirm token references match the color and spacing tokens defined in subtasks 3 and 4. Cross-check with Task 47 component gallery requirements.

subtasks.title: Generate Page-Specific Design Spec Files
subtasks.description: Create individual design spec markdown files in design-system/pages/ for all key application pages including profile, team, auth, and others.
subtasks.details: Create `design-system/pages/` directory with the following spec files: (1) `auth.md` — login, register, forgot-password layouts, background pattern, branded card, form states (loading/error/success), P.I.T logo placement; (2) `profile.md` — hero section, stats grid, matches DataTable, settings forms, breadcrumb navigation, sub-page layouts (/profile/matches, /profile/settings); (3) `team.md` — team roster layout, position grid, team stats, management actions; (4) `tournaments.md` — tournament list, bracket view, registration flow; (5) `landing.md` — hero, features, CTA sections; (6) `dashboard.md` — overview widgets, activity feed, quick actions. Each file should reference MASTER.md tokens, specify component usage, define responsive breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop), and include empty/loading/error state designs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all page spec files exist in design-system/pages/. Confirm each file references MASTER.md tokens correctly. Cross-check auth.md against Task 32 requirements and profile.md against Task 34 requirements. Validate page specs cover all routes in the application.

subtasks.title: Manual Validation, Primary Color Decision, and Anti-Pattern Documentation
subtasks.description: Conduct a design review checkpoint, finalize the primary color decision (blue vs orange), validate WCAG AA compliance, and document anti-patterns in MASTER.md.
subtasks.details: Perform a comprehensive review: (1) Primary color finalization — compare blue (#0d7ff2) vs orange side-by-side in context of esports/gaming aesthetic and brand identity; document the decision rationale in MASTER.md header; update all color references accordingly; (2) WCAG AA validation — run WAVE tool on all color combinations, verify 4.5:1 contrast for text and 3:1 for UI components, document results; (3) Anti-patterns section in MASTER.md — list prohibited patterns: hardcoded hex colors (use CSS vars), rgba() without tokens, inconsistent spacing (non-4px-grid values), missing focus-visible styles, inline styles overriding tokens; (4) Completeness checklist — validate MASTER.md against a checklist: color palette ✓, typography ✓, spacing ✓, all 6 components ✓, all page specs ✓, anti-patterns ✓; (5) Design review checkpoint — schedule/document sign-off from stakeholders before proceeding to Task 29.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run WAVE tool on all documented color combinations and confirm WCAG AA pass. Verify MASTER.md completeness checklist is 100% complete. Confirm primary color decision is documented with rationale. Verify anti-patterns section lists at least 5 prohibited patterns. Ensure design-system/ is ready to unblock Task 29 (Implement Design Tokens).

tasks.expansionPrompt: Structure design system generation into phases: (1) UI/UX Pro Max skill installation and configuration, (2) Design reference collection (screenshots from VirtualNexusArena, 21st.dev, Dribbble), (3) MASTER.md generation with color palette section, (4) Typography and spacing scale definition, (5) Component specification (button, card, modal, table, sidebar, navbar), (6) Page-specific design specs (profile.md, team.md, auth.md, etc.), (7) Manual validation, color decision finalization, and anti-pattern documentation. Include design review checkpoints.

tasks.title: Implement Design Tokens in CSS/Tailwind Config
tasks.description: Apply MASTER.md tokens to globals.css, tailwind.config.ts, layout.tsx, convert hardcoded styles to CSS variables
tasks.details: 1. Update src/app/globals.css: add --card-hover, --surface-1/2, --position-gk/def/mid/fwd/atk tokens from MASTER.md. 2. tailwind.config.ts: extend theme with typography scale (page-title, section-title, etc.), spacing scale. 3. src/app/layout.tsx: next/font/google for main font + Fira Code (.font-data utility). 4. Replace .nav-item-active #0d7ff2 → hsl(var(--primary)). 5. Replace .glass-sidebar rgba() → CSS var tokens. 6. Add focus-visible ring styles for WCAG. 7. Create tokens audit script to find remaining hardcoded values.
tasks.testStrategy: Run tokens audit script (0 hardcoded colors), Lighthouse accessibility 90+, keyboard navigation test (Tab/Enter/Esc), contrast check with browser devtools

subtasks.title: Define Semantic CSS Variables in globals.css
subtasks.description: Add all missing design token CSS variables to src/app/globals.css, including --card-hover, --surface-1, --surface-2, and position tokens (--position-gk, --position-def, --position-mid, --position-fwd, --position-atk) from MASTER.md, organized under :root and dark mode selectors.
subtasks.details: Open src/app/globals.css and add the following token groups under :root: (1) Surface tokens: --surface-1, --surface-2 with appropriate HSL values. (2) Interaction tokens: --card-hover with hover state color. (3) Position tokens: --position-gk, --position-def, --position-mid, --position-fwd, --position-atk each with distinct HSL color values matching MASTER.md spec. Ensure dark mode overrides are placed inside a [data-theme='dark'] or .dark selector block. Maintain alphabetical/grouped ordering for maintainability. Cross-reference MASTER.md for exact token values and naming conventions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Visually inspect token values in browser DevTools under :root computed styles. Run the token audit script (subtask 7) to confirm all MASTER.md tokens are present. Check dark mode toggle applies correct overrides.

subtasks.title: Extend Tailwind Config with Typography Scale
subtasks.description: Update tailwind.config.ts to extend the theme with a custom typography scale including page-title, section-title, card-title, body-lg, body-sm, label, and data-value font size/line-height/weight definitions.
subtasks.details: In tailwind.config.ts, under theme.extend.fontSize (or theme.extend.typography), add named entries: page-title (e.g., ['2rem', { lineHeight: '2.5rem', fontWeight: '700' }]), section-title (e.g., ['1.5rem', { lineHeight: '2rem', fontWeight: '600' }]), card-title (e.g., ['1.125rem', { lineHeight: '1.75rem', fontWeight: '600' }]), body-lg, body-sm, label, data-value. Reference MASTER.md for exact scale values. Ensure these map to CSS variable references where applicable (e.g., var(--font-size-page-title)) for runtime theming flexibility. Export types if using TypeScript strict mode.
subtasks.testStrategy: Apply a Tailwind class like text-page-title to a test element and verify correct font-size/line-height in browser. Run `npm run build` to confirm no Tailwind config errors.

subtasks.title: Extend Tailwind Config with Spacing Scale
subtasks.description: Add a custom spacing scale to tailwind.config.ts that maps to design token values from MASTER.md, ensuring consistent spacing across all components and pages.
subtasks.details: In tailwind.config.ts under theme.extend.spacing, add named spacing tokens that align with MASTER.md (e.g., section-gap, card-padding, sidebar-width, header-height, etc.). Use numeric or semantic keys as appropriate. Where spacing tokens reference CSS variables (e.g., var(--spacing-section-gap)), use a CSS variable reference pattern compatible with Tailwind's JIT engine. Also extend theme.extend.borderRadius, theme.extend.boxShadow if MASTER.md defines shadow/radius tokens. Document each token with an inline comment indicating its intended use context.
subtasks.testStrategy: Apply spacing utility classes in a test component and verify correct pixel values in DevTools. Run `npm run build` to confirm no config errors. Cross-check spacing values against MASTER.md.

subtasks.title: Configure Fonts via next/font/google in layout.tsx
subtasks.description: Set up the primary application font and Fira Code monospace font using next/font/google in src/app/layout.tsx, exposing them as CSS variables and a .font-data utility class.
subtasks.details: In src/app/layout.tsx: (1) Import the primary font (e.g., Inter or the font specified in MASTER.md) from 'next/font/google' with variable: '--font-sans' and appropriate subsets/weights. (2) Import Fira_Code from 'next/font/google' with variable: '--font-mono' and subsets. (3) Apply both font variables to the <html> or <body> element via className. (4) In globals.css, define .font-data { font-family: var(--font-mono); } utility class. (5) Update tailwind.config.ts fontFamily to reference var(--font-sans) and var(--font-mono). Ensure no layout shift by using display: 'swap'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify fonts load correctly in Network tab (no FOUT). Inspect computed font-family on body and on an element with .font-data class. Run Lighthouse performance check to confirm no font-related CLS.

subtasks.title: Audit and Replace Hardcoded Colors with CSS Variable Tokens
subtasks.description: Systematically find and replace all hardcoded color values in the codebase with their corresponding CSS variable tokens, starting with high-impact targets: .nav-item-active (#0d7ff2 → hsl(var(--primary))) and .glass-sidebar (rgba() values → CSS var tokens).
subtasks.details: Step 1: Use grep or the audit script (subtask 7) to locate all hardcoded hex, rgb(), rgba(), and hsl() values in .css, .tsx, .ts files under src/. Step 2: Replace .nav-item-active color #0d7ff2 with hsl(var(--primary)) in the relevant CSS/component file. Step 3: Replace .glass-sidebar rgba() background/border values with appropriate CSS variable tokens (e.g., var(--surface-1), var(--card-hover), var(--border)). Step 4: Replace any other high-frequency hardcoded values (e.g., bg-gray-950 in utility pages per task 44). Step 5: Verify visual consistency after each replacement. Maintain a changelog of all replacements made.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the token audit script and confirm 0 hardcoded color violations remain. Visual regression check on nav and sidebar components. Run `npm run build` to confirm no broken references.

subtasks.title: Implement Focus-Visible Ring Styles for WCAG Accessibility
subtasks.description: Add global focus-visible ring styles to globals.css and Tailwind config to ensure all interactive elements meet WCAG AA keyboard navigation and focus indicator requirements.
subtasks.details: In globals.css, add a global rule: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--primary)); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius, 4px); } to replace browser defaults. Ensure the ring color meets 3:1 contrast ratio against all background surfaces per WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11. In tailwind.config.ts, add a ring-primary utility or extend ring colors to reference var(--primary). Add focus-visible variants for custom components (buttons, inputs, nav items, cards). Remove any outline: none or outline: 0 declarations that suppress focus indicators without providing alternatives. Test with keyboard-only navigation (Tab, Enter, Esc).
subtasks.testStrategy: Keyboard navigation test: Tab through all interactive elements and verify visible focus ring on each. Run Lighthouse accessibility audit targeting 90+ score. Use browser DevTools contrast checker to verify ring color contrast ratio ≥ 3:1.

subtasks.title: Create Token Audit Script for Hardcoded Value Detection
subtasks.description: Build a Node.js/shell script that scans the codebase for remaining hardcoded color, spacing, and typography values, reporting violations with file paths and line numbers to enforce design token adoption.
subtasks.details: Create scripts/token-audit.ts (or .mjs) that: (1) Recursively scans src/ for .tsx, .ts, .css files. (2) Uses regex patterns to detect hardcoded hex colors (#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}), rgb()/rgba() calls, hsl() calls not wrapping a CSS variable, hardcoded pixel font sizes (font-size: Npx), and Tailwind color classes with hardcoded palette values (e.g., bg-gray-950, text-blue-500). (3) Outputs a formatted report: file path, line number, matched value, suggested token replacement. (4) Exits with code 1 if violations are found (for CI integration). (5) Add an npm script entry: "token-audit": "tsx scripts/token-audit.ts" in package.json. Include an allowlist for intentional exceptions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run token-audit` on the codebase before and after subtask 5 refactoring. Verify the script correctly identifies known hardcoded values and reports 0 violations after all replacements. Test CI exit code behavior by introducing a deliberate hardcoded value.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break token implementation into systematic subtasks: (1) CSS variable definition in globals.css (semantic tokens), (2) Tailwind config theme extension (typography scale), (3) Tailwind config spacing scale, (4) Font setup via next/font/google, (5) Hardcoded color replacement audit and refactoring, (6) Focus-visible and accessibility ring styles, (7) Token audit script creation and validation. Prioritize by page/component impact.

tasks.title: Shared Components Library Expansion

All components: WCAG AA contrast, keyboard nav (Tab/Enter/Esc), aria-labels on icons, focus-visible ring. 4. Use Fira Code for numeric data via .font-data.
tasks.testStrategy: Component Storybook/isolated tests for all states (default/loading/disabled/hover/error), keyboard navigation test, contrast analysis, `npm run build` passes

subtasks.title: Install and Configure shadcn/ui Components
subtasks.description: Run the shadcn CLI to install all required base components and verify they integrate correctly with the existing project setup.
subtasks.details: Execute `npx shadcn@latest add dialog dropdown-menu avatar tooltip sheet breadcrumb skeleton scroll-area progress` in the project root. Verify each component is scaffolded under the correct path (typically src/components/ui/). Confirm tailwind.config.js and globals.css are updated with shadcn CSS variables. Resolve any peer dependency conflicts. Ensure the project still builds successfully with `npm run build` after installation. Document any configuration overrides needed for the project's design tokens.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build` to confirm no build errors. Import each installed shadcn component in a test file to verify exports are correct. Check that CSS variables are present in the compiled stylesheet.

subtasks.title: Build PageHeader Component
subtasks.description: Create a reusable PageHeader component supporting title, subtitle, and an actions slot for buttons or controls.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/PageHeader.tsx`. Props: `title: string`, `subtitle?: string`, `actions?: React.ReactNode`. Use semantic HTML (`<header>`, `<h1>`). Apply CSS design tokens for typography and spacing (no hardcoded colors). Ensure the actions slot is right-aligned on desktop and stacks below on mobile. Add `aria-label` to the header element. Implement focus-visible ring on any interactive elements within actions. Add a Storybook story with variants: title only, title+subtitle, title+subtitle+actions. Use Fira Code font class `.font-data` for any numeric content in subtitle if applicable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories for all prop combinations. Axe accessibility audit in Storybook. Keyboard navigation test: Tab through action buttons. Verify WCAG AA contrast ratios for title and subtitle text.

subtasks.title: Build StatCard v2 Component with Sparkline
subtasks.description: Create an upgraded StatCard component supporting icon, label, value, and an optional sparkline chart.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/StatCard.tsx`. Props: `icon: React.ReactNode`, `label: string`, `value: string | number`, `sparklineData?: number[]`, `trend?: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'`. Use `.font-data` (Fira Code) for the value display. Integrate a lightweight sparkline (e.g., recharts Sparkline or a custom SVG path). Apply CSS tokens for background, border, and text colors. Add `aria-label` to the icon wrapper. Ensure the card is keyboard-focusable if it is interactive. Provide loading skeleton state via the shadcn Skeleton component. Add Storybook stories: default, with sparkline, loading, trend up/down.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories for all variants. Verify `.font-data` class is applied to value. Axe audit for icon aria-labels. Contrast check on trend color indicators. Snapshot test for sparkline rendering.

subtasks.title: Build EmptyState, ErrorState, and NoPermissionState Components
subtasks.description: Create three state-feedback components for empty data, error conditions, and permission denial, each with consistent structure and accessibility.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/EmptyState.tsx`, `ErrorState.tsx`, and `NoPermissionState.tsx`. Shared props pattern: `title: string`, `description?: string`, `action?: { label: string; onClick: () => void }`. EmptyState: neutral illustration/icon, call-to-action button. ErrorState: error icon (red, WCAG AA), retry action. NoPermissionState: lock icon, contact admin message. All use `role='status'` or `role='alert'` as appropriate. Icons must have `aria-hidden` with visible text labels. Action buttons must be keyboard accessible with focus-visible ring. Add Storybook stories for each variant with and without action.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories for all three components. Axe audit per component. Keyboard test: Tab to action button, Enter triggers callback. Contrast ratio check on error red and icon colors.

subtasks.title: Build LoadingSkeleton Component with Multiple Variants
subtasks.description: Create a LoadingSkeleton component with variants matching the shapes of key UI elements: card, table row, text block, avatar, and stat card.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/LoadingSkeleton.tsx`. Use the shadcn Skeleton primitive as the base. Variants: `card` (rectangular block with header+body), `tableRow` (multi-column row), `textBlock` (multiple lines of varying width), `avatar` (circle), `statCard` (icon+label+value layout). Props: `variant: 'card' | 'tableRow' | 'textBlock' | 'avatar' | 'statCard'`, `rows?: number` (for tableRow/textBlock). Add `aria-busy='true'` and `aria-label='Loading...'` on the wrapper. Animate with the shadcn pulse animation. Add Storybook stories for each variant.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories for all 5 variants. Verify aria-busy attribute is present. Confirm animation runs via visual inspection in Storybook. Axe audit to ensure no accessibility violations.

subtasks.title: Build StatusBadge Component with State Variants
subtasks.description: Create a StatusBadge component supporting approved, pending, rejected, and extensible custom states with WCAG AA compliant color coding.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/StatusBadge.tsx`. Props: `status: 'approved' | 'pending' | 'rejected' | string`, `label?: string` (defaults to capitalized status). Use CSS tokens for colors: approved=green, pending=amber, rejected=red — all must pass WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 minimum) against both light and dark backgrounds. Add a colored dot indicator alongside the label text. Use `role='status'` and ensure the text is always visible (not icon-only). Add Storybook stories for all four states. Support size variants: `sm` and `md`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories for approved/pending/rejected/custom. Run contrast checker on all color combinations. Axe audit. Verify role='status' is present. Visual regression screenshot per variant.

subtasks.title: Build ScoreDisplay and PlayerAvatar Components
subtasks.description: Create ScoreDisplay for match scores with home/away formatting and PlayerAvatar for player profile images with fallback initials.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/ScoreDisplay.tsx` and `src/components/shared/PlayerAvatar.tsx`. ScoreDisplay props: `homeScore: number`, `awayScore: number`, `status?: 'live' | 'final' | 'upcoming'`. Use `.font-data` (Fira Code) for score numbers. Highlight winning side. Add `aria-label` like 'Home 3, Away 1, Final'. PlayerAvatar: wraps shadcn Avatar. Props: `src?: string`, `name: string`, `size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'`. Fallback to initials (first+last name chars) when image fails. Add `alt` text with player name. Support `online` status indicator dot. Add Storybook stories for both components covering all states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook stories: ScoreDisplay (live/final/upcoming, win/draw/loss). PlayerAvatar (with image, without image/initials fallback, sizes). Axe audit. Verify .font-data on scores. Test image error fallback behavior.

subtasks.title: Build DataTable Component with Sorting, Filtering, and Pagination
subtasks.description: Create a fully featured DataTable component supporting column sorting, text filtering, and client-side pagination with full keyboard accessibility.
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx`. Use shadcn's table primitives and integrate TanStack Table (react-table v8) for headless logic. Props: `columns: ColumnDef[]`, `data: T[]`, `pageSize?: number`, `searchable?: boolean`, `searchPlaceholder?: string`. Features: clickable column headers for sort (asc/desc/none cycle) with aria-sort attribute, global text filter input, pagination controls (prev/next/page numbers). Use shadcn ScrollArea for horizontal overflow. All interactive elements (sort headers, pagination buttons) must be keyboard accessible (Tab/Enter/Space). Add loading state that renders LoadingSkeleton tableRow variant. Add EmptyState when no results. Add Storybook story with mock data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Storybook story with 50-row mock dataset. Keyboard test: Tab to column header, Enter to sort, Tab to filter input, Tab to pagination. Verify aria-sort updates on sort toggle. Axe audit. Test empty state renders when filter matches nothing. Test pagination page count math.

subtasks.title: WCAG AA Validation, Keyboard Navigation Testing, and Storybook Setup
subtasks.description: Set up Storybook for the shared component library and perform comprehensive WCAG AA compliance and keyboard navigation validation across all components built in this task.
subtasks.details: If Storybook is not already configured, run `npx storybook@latest init` and configure it for the Next.js/React project. Ensure all components from subtasks 2–8 have complete Storybook stories covering default, loading, disabled, hover, and error states. Run `axe-core` or `@storybook/addon-a11y` against every story and resolve all WCAG AA violations (contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text). Manually test keyboard navigation flows: Tab order, Enter/Space activation, Escape to close modals/dropdowns, focus-visible ring visibility on all interactive elements. Verify `.font-data` (Fira Code) is applied consistently to all numeric displays. Document any known limitations. Ensure `npm run build` passes with all new components included.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `@storybook/addon-a11y` and confirm zero violations across all stories. Manual keyboard walkthrough checklist per component. Use browser DevTools color contrast analyzer on all text/background pairs. Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript or build errors. Generate a contrast audit report.

Include Storybook setup.

subtasks.title: Material Symbols to Lucide React Icon Migration

subtasks.details: 1. Audit all Material Symbols icon usages across src/components/layout/SidebarShell.tsx and any other layout files. 2. Create a mapping table of Material Symbol names → Lucide React icon component names. 3. Replace each icon import and JSX usage with the corresponding Lucide React component (e.g., menu → from lucide-react). 4. Uninstall the material-symbols package from package.json and remove any CSS imports referencing it. 5. Verify no other files in the project depend on material-symbols before removal. 6. Run bundle size analysis (e.g., using `next build` + bundle analyzer) to confirm size reduction. 7.
