subtasks.title: Replace Hardcoded Slate Colors with CSS Design Tokens
subtasks.description: Audit and replace all hardcoded Tailwind slate-* color classes and any inline hex/rgb color values in SidebarShell.tsx and related layout components with the project's CSS custom property tokens.
subtasks.details: 1. Identify all hardcoded color references in src/components/layout/SidebarShell.tsx (e.g., bg-slate-800, text-slate-400, border-slate-700, etc.). 2. Cross-reference the project's CSS token definitions (e.g., --color-sidebar-bg, --color-text-muted, --color-border) from the design system tokens file. 3. Replace each hardcoded Tailwind class with the corresponding CSS token utility class or inline CSS variable (e.g., bg-slate-800 → bg-[var(--color-sidebar-bg)]). 4. Ensure dark/light mode variants are handled via the token system rather than hardcoded values. 5. Update any inline style objects that reference hardcoded colors. 6. Document any new tokens that need to be added to the token file if a mapping doesn't exist.
subtasks.testStrategy: Toggle between light and dark mode and verify sidebar/navbar colors update correctly via tokens. Use browser DevTools to confirm CSS variables are applied. Run visual regression screenshots before and after to confirm no unintended color changes.

subtasks.title: Collapsible Sidebar State Management with localStorage Persistence
subtasks.description: Implement a collapsible sidebar toggle in SidebarShell.tsx with state persisted to localStorage so the user's preference survives page reloads and is consistent across tabs.
subtasks.details: 1. Add a React state variable `isCollapsed` (boolean) initialized by reading from localStorage key (e.g., 'sidebar-collapsed') on mount using a custom hook or useEffect. 2. Implement a toggle button (using a Lucide icon such as PanelLeftClose/PanelLeftOpen) in the sidebar header. 3. On toggle, update React state and write the new value to localStorage. 4. Apply conditional CSS classes to the sidebar container to animate between expanded (e.g., w-64) and collapsed (e.g., w-16) states using CSS transitions. 5. In collapsed state, hide text labels and show only icons with tooltips for accessibility. 6. Use a BroadcastChannel or storage event listener to sync collapse state across browser tabs. 7. Ensure the main content area adjusts its left margin/padding responsively when sidebar collapses.
subtasks.testStrategy: Toggle sidebar, reload page, and verify state persists. Open two tabs and verify collapse state syncs. Test keyboard accessibility of toggle button. Verify smooth CSS transition animation. Check that main content area reflows correctly on collapse/expand.

subtasks.title: Mobile Drawer Implementation via shadcn Sheet
subtasks.description: Implement a mobile-friendly sidebar drawer using the shadcn/ui Sheet component that slides in from the left on small screens, replacing the persistent sidebar on mobile viewports.
subtasks.details: 1. Install/confirm shadcn Sheet component is available in the project. 2. Add a hamburger menu button (Lucide Menu icon) in the navbar visible only on mobile (md:hidden). 3. Manage drawer open/close state with useState in SidebarShell or a parent layout component. 4. Wrap the sidebar navigation content in a shadcn <Sheet> with side='left', rendering the same nav items as the desktop sidebar. 5. On mobile (< md breakpoint), hide the persistent sidebar (hidden md:flex) and show the Sheet-based drawer instead. 6. Ensure the Sheet closes on navigation link click (add onClick handler to nav items that calls setOpen(false)). 7. Implement focus trap and keyboard escape to close the drawer per accessibility standards. 8. Apply the same CSS token colors to the Sheet content for visual consistency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test on mobile viewport (375px, 768px) in browser DevTools. Verify hamburger button appears only on mobile. Open drawer, click a nav link, and confirm drawer closes. Test keyboard navigation: Tab through items, Escape closes drawer. Run visual regression screenshots at mobile breakpoints.

subtasks.title: Navbar Breadcrumb and User Avatar DropdownMenu
subtasks.description: Enhance the top navbar in SidebarShell with a dynamic Breadcrumb component reflecting the current route and a user avatar DropdownMenu with profile/logout actions.
subtasks.details: 1. Integrate shadcn Breadcrumb component into the navbar. Use Next.js usePathname() to derive breadcrumb segments dynamically from the current route. Map path segments to human-readable labels (e.g., /profile/settings → Home > Profile > Settings). 2. Render breadcrumb items with separator icons (Lucide ChevronRight). Make all but the last segment clickable links. 3. Add a user avatar button on the right side of the navbar using shadcn DropdownMenu. Display user initials or avatar image from auth context. 4. DropdownMenu items: View Profile (links to /profile), Settings (links to /profile/settings), divider, Sign Out (calls auth signOut). 5. Use Lucide icons (User, Settings, LogOut) for dropdown items. 6. Apply CSS token colors to all new navbar elements. 7. Ensure dropdown is keyboard accessible (arrow keys, Enter, Escape).
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to several routes and verify breadcrumb updates correctly. Click breadcrumb links and confirm navigation. Open user dropdown with mouse and keyboard. Click Sign Out and verify auth flow. Test on mobile to ensure navbar doesn't overflow. Run visual regression screenshots.

subtasks.title: Landing Layout Variant Without Sidebar
subtasks.description: Create a separate layout variant for landing/marketing pages that renders without the sidebar, providing a full-width content area with a simplified navbar.
subtasks.details: 1. Create a new layout component src/components/layout/LandingShell.tsx (or a variant prop on SidebarShell). 2. The landing layout should render only the top navbar (without breadcrumb, with a simplified logo + CTA links) and a full-width main content area. 3. Optionally accept a prop `variant='landing'` on SidebarShell to conditionally hide the sidebar and adjust navbar content, or create a fully separate component for cleaner separation. 4. Apply CSS token backgrounds and spacing consistent with the design system. 5. Ensure the landing layout is used in Next.js route groups (e.g., (landing) route group) by exporting the appropriate layout.tsx. 6. The landing navbar should include the P.I.T logo/brand and navigation links (e.g., Login, Register CTAs). 7. Verify no sidebar-related state (collapsible, localStorage) is initialized in the landing layout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to a landing route and verify no sidebar renders. Confirm full-width layout. Check that sidebar localStorage state is not read/written on landing pages. Test mobile responsiveness of landing navbar. Run visual regression screenshots for landing layout.

subtasks.title: Responsive Design Testing and Visual Regression at 1440p/1080p/1366px Breakpoints
subtasks.description: Validate the complete redesigned layout shell across all required breakpoints (1440p, 1080p, 1366px) and establish visual regression screenshot baselines to prevent future regressions.
subtasks.details: 1. Set up or configure a visual regression testing tool (e.g., Playwright with screenshot comparison, Storybook Chromatic, or Percy). 2. Write test scenarios for: (a) Desktop sidebar expanded at 1440px, 1080px, 1366px widths; (b) Desktop sidebar collapsed at each breakpoint; (c) Mobile drawer open/closed at 375px and 768px; (d) Navbar with breadcrumb at each breakpoint; (e) Landing layout at each breakpoint. 3. Capture baseline screenshots for all scenarios. 4. Verify sidebar, navbar, and content area proportions are correct at each breakpoint — no overflow, no clipped text, no broken layouts. 5. Validate CSS token colors render correctly in both light and dark mode at each breakpoint. 6. Check keyboard navigation flow (Tab order) across sidebar, navbar, and main content at each breakpoint. 7. Document any breakpoint-specific CSS adjustments needed and apply fixes. 8. Integrate visual regression tests into CI pipeline to run on PRs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Playwright (or equivalent) screenshot tests at 1440x900, 1920x1080, and 1366x768 viewport sizes. Compare against baselines and assert zero pixel diff (or within threshold). Manually QA on physical devices or BrowserStack. Verify WCAG AA color contrast ratios using axe-core or Lighthouse at each breakpoint.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose layout redesign into focused subtasks: (1) Material Symbols to Lucide React icon migration, (2) Hardcoded color replacement with CSS tokens, (3) Collapsible sidebar state management and localStorage persistence, (4) Mobile drawer implementation via shadcn Sheet, (5) Navbar with Breadcrumb and user DropdownMenu, (6) Landing layout variant (no sidebar), (7) Responsive design testing (1440p/1080p/1366px breakpoints). Include visual regression testing.

tasks.title: Auth Pages Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /login, /register, /forgot-password with background pattern, branded cards, consistent states (loading/error/success)
tasks.details: 1. Apply design-system/pages/auth.md spec. 2. Add subtle grid/texture background via CSS. 3. Auth cards use new tokens + branding (P.I.T logo). 4. Consistent layout across 3 pages. 5. States: loading skeleton, error boundary, success redirect. 6. Form validation with shadcn Input/Label. 7. WCAG AA keyboard focus.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all 3 pages with real Supabase auth flow, loading/error/success states, keyboard form navigation, mobile responsiveness, visual consistency check

subtasks.title: Apply Design Spec from design-system/pages/auth.md
subtasks.description: Parse and implement all design tokens, layout rules, spacing, typography, and color specifications defined in design-system/pages/auth.md across all three auth pages.
subtasks.details: Read design-system/pages/auth.md thoroughly and extract all relevant tokens (colors, spacing, border-radius, font sizes, shadows). Create a shared auth layout wrapper component (e.g., AuthLayout) that enforces the spec's page structure. Map all design tokens to Tailwind/CSS variables already defined in the design system. Document any deviations or missing tokens that need to be added. Ensure the layout wrapper is reusable across /login, /register, and /forgot-password pages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Visual diff against design-system/pages/auth.md mockups; verify all CSS variables/tokens are correctly applied using browser DevTools; cross-check spacing and typography with the spec document.

subtasks.title: Implement Background Pattern/Texture CSS
subtasks.description: Create a subtle grid or texture background effect using pure CSS that is applied globally to all auth pages via the shared AuthLayout component.
subtasks.details: Implement the background pattern using CSS (e.g., repeating-linear-gradient, SVG data URI, or CSS grid dot pattern) as specified in auth.md. Add the background styles to the AuthLayout wrapper or a dedicated CSS module/Tailwind utility class. Ensure the pattern is subtle and does not interfere with readability. The background must be responsive and render correctly on all screen sizes. Test for performance — avoid heavy image assets; prefer CSS-only or lightweight SVG solutions. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by providing a fallback plain background.
subtasks.testStrategy: Visual inspection across breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop); verify pattern renders in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari; check prefers-reduced-motion fallback; Lighthouse performance check to ensure no render-blocking assets.

subtasks.title: Build Reusable Auth Card Component with P.I.T Branding
subtasks.description: Create a branded AuthCard component that includes the P.I.T logo, consistent card styling using design tokens, and serves as the container for all auth forms.
subtasks.details: Build an AuthCard React component using shadcn Card primitives styled with the new design tokens (background, border, shadow, border-radius). Integrate the P.I.T logo at the top of the card (SVG or Next.js Image component). The card should accept a title prop, optional subtitle, and children for form content. Apply consistent padding, max-width constraints, and center alignment. Ensure the card is visually distinct from the background pattern. Add aria-labelledby for accessibility. Export from a shared components/auth/ directory for reuse across all three pages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render AuthCard in isolation (Storybook or test page); verify logo renders correctly at all sizes; check aria attributes with axe-core or browser accessibility inspector; test card layout on mobile (375px) and desktop (1280px).

subtasks.title: Redesign /login Page with Form Validation and All States
subtasks.description: Implement the redesigned /login page using AuthLayout, AuthCard, shadcn Input/Label components, form validation, and all UI states (loading, error, success).
subtasks.details: Rebuild the /login page using the AuthLayout and AuthCard components. Use shadcn Input and Label for email and password fields with react-hook-form or native validation. Implement client-side validation (required fields, email format, password min length). Connect to Supabase auth (signInWithPassword). Handle loading state with a button spinner or disabled state. Display inline error messages for validation failures and Supabase auth errors (e.g., invalid credentials). On success, redirect to the appropriate dashboard route. Add a link to /forgot-password and /register. Ensure full keyboard navigation (tab order, enter to submit). Apply WCAG AA focus ring styles.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with valid/invalid credentials against real Supabase; verify loading spinner appears during async call; verify error messages display for wrong password and unregistered email; test keyboard-only navigation through the form; test on mobile viewport (375px).

subtasks.title: Redesign /register Page with Form Validation and All States
subtasks.description: Implement the redesigned /register page using AuthLayout, AuthCard, shadcn Input/Label components, form validation, and all UI states (loading, error, success).
subtasks.details: Rebuild the /register page using AuthLayout and AuthCard. Include fields for email, password, and confirm password (and any additional required fields per spec). Use shadcn Input/Label with react-hook-form validation: required fields, email format, password strength, password match confirmation. Connect to Supabase auth (signUp). Show loading state during submission. Display field-level and form-level error messages including Supabase errors (e.g., email already in use). On success, show a success message or redirect as per the auth flow spec. Add a link to /login. Ensure WCAG AA keyboard focus and tab order. Apply consistent styling matching /login page.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test registration with new and existing email addresses; verify password mismatch error; verify Supabase error messages surface correctly; test keyboard navigation; test on mobile viewport; visual consistency check against /login page.

subtasks.title: Redesign /forgot-password Page with Form Validation and All States
subtasks.description: Implement the redesigned /forgot-password page using AuthLayout, AuthCard, shadcn Input/Label, form validation, and all UI states (loading, error, success).
subtasks.details: Rebuild the /forgot-password page using AuthLayout and AuthCard. Include a single email input field with shadcn Input/Label. Validate email format on submit. Connect to Supabase auth (resetPasswordForEmail). Show loading state during the async call. On success, display a success message (e.g., 'Check your email for a reset link') without redirecting. Display error messages for invalid email or Supabase errors. Add a back link to /login. Ensure WCAG AA keyboard focus and accessible error/success announcements via aria-live regions. Match visual style of /login and /register pages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with valid and invalid email addresses; verify success message appears after Supabase call; verify error states; test aria-live region announces success/error to screen readers; keyboard navigation test; mobile viewport test; visual consistency check against other auth pages.

subtasks.title: Loading/Error/Success State Handling, Supabase Auth Flow Integration, and Accessibility/Responsiveness QA
subtasks.description: Implement shared loading skeleton, error boundary, and success redirect utilities for all auth pages, finalize Supabase auth flow integration, and conduct full keyboard navigation and mobile responsiveness QA.
subtasks.details: Create a shared LoadingSkeleton component for auth cards (pulsing placeholder matching card dimensions). Implement an ErrorBoundary wrapper for auth pages to catch unexpected runtime errors gracefully. Audit and finalize Supabase auth flow across all three pages: ensure session handling, redirect logic, and error code mapping are consistent. Conduct WCAG AA keyboard focus audit: verify visible focus rings on all interactive elements, correct tab order, and no focus traps. Test mobile responsiveness at 375px, 768px, and 1280px breakpoints. Fix any layout overflow, font scaling, or touch target issues. Run axe-core or Lighthouse accessibility audit and resolve all critical issues. Perform a final visual consistency review across all three auth pages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full Supabase auth flow (login, register, forgot-password) end-to-end in staging environment; keyboard-only navigation test on all three pages; axe-core accessibility scan with zero critical violations; Lighthouse mobile score 90+; cross-browser test (Chrome, Firefox, Safari); visual regression snapshot comparison across all three pages.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Structure auth pages redesign into subtasks: (1) Design spec application from design-system/pages/auth.md, (2) Background pattern/texture CSS implementation, (3) Auth card component with branding (P.I.T logo), (4) /login page with form validation and states, (5) /register page with form validation and states, (6) /forgot-password page with form validation and states, (7) Loading/error/success state handling and Supabase auth flow integration. Include keyboard navigation and mobile responsiveness testing.

tasks.title: Profile Page Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /profile using page spec: PlayerHeader, StatsOverview (StatCard v2), EvolutionChart, MatchHistory (DataTable), all states
tasks.details: 1. Follow design-system/pages/profile.md. 2. PlayerHeader: larger PlayerAvatar + PositionBadge. 3. StatsOverview: StatCard v2 grid + trends. 4. EvolutionChart: branded colors + tooltip. 5. MatchHistory: DataTable sorting. 6. PositionStats: visual map/spider chart. 7. PendingInvitesCard/QuickClubRefreshCard with StatusBadge. 8. Suspense + LoadingSkeleton per section.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with real player data + empty data, all states (loading/empty/error), DataTable sort/filter, chart interactions, Lighthouse 90+ on page

subtasks.title: Implement PlayerHeader with Larger Avatar and PositionBadge
subtasks.description: Build the PlayerHeader component featuring an enlarged PlayerAvatar and a PositionBadge, following the profile page spec in design-system/pages/profile.md.
subtasks.details: 1. Create or update PlayerHeader component in the profile page directory. 2. Integrate PlayerAvatar at a larger size (e.g., 96px or as specified in design-system/pages/profile.md). 3. Add PositionBadge component alongside the avatar, using design tokens for colors and typography. 4. Ensure the header displays player name, position, and any relevant metadata (e.g., club, rating). 5. Apply responsive layout so the header adapts to mobile and desktop breakpoints. 6. Export and integrate PlayerHeader into the /profile page layout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render PlayerHeader with real player data and verify avatar size, PositionBadge display, and responsive behavior. Test with missing/empty player data to confirm graceful fallback rendering.

subtasks.title: Build StatsOverview with StatCard v2 Grid and Trend Indicators
subtasks.description: Implement the StatsOverview section using the StatCard v2 component in a responsive grid layout, including trend indicators for each stat.
subtasks.details: 1. Create or update StatCard v2 component with support for trend indicators (up/down/neutral arrows or percentage deltas). 2. Compose StatsOverview as a grid of StatCard v2 instances, following the column/row spec in design-system/pages/profile.md. 3. Map player stats data (goals, assists, matches played, etc.) to individual StatCard v2 props. 4. Apply design tokens for colors, spacing, and typography. 5. Ensure the grid is responsive (e.g., 2-col on mobile, 4-col on desktop). 6. Integrate StatsOverview into the /profile page below PlayerHeader.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render StatsOverview with real stats data and verify grid layout, trend indicator direction/color, and correct stat values. Test with empty/null stats to confirm empty state handling.

subtasks.title: Implement EvolutionChart with Branded Colors and Tooltip
subtasks.description: Build the EvolutionChart component using branded design-system colors and an interactive tooltip to display player performance over time.
subtasks.details: 1. Select or configure a charting library (e.g., Recharts or Chart.js) consistent with the project stack. 2. Apply branded color palette from design tokens for chart lines, fills, and axes. 3. Implement an interactive tooltip that displays date, metric name, and value on hover/focus. 4. Map player evolution data (e.g., rating over time, goals per month) to chart data format. 5. Ensure the chart is accessible (keyboard navigable, ARIA labels). 6. Add responsive sizing so the chart fills its container on all breakpoints. 7. Integrate EvolutionChart into the /profile page layout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render EvolutionChart with real time-series data and verify branded colors, tooltip content on hover, and responsive resizing. Test with empty data array to confirm empty/no-data state is displayed correctly.

subtasks.title: Build MatchHistory DataTable with Sorting
subtasks.description: Implement the MatchHistory section using the DataTable component with column sorting capabilities, displaying the player's recent match records.
subtasks.details: 1. Configure DataTable with columns for match date, opponent, result, goals, assists, and rating. 2. Implement client-side or server-side column sorting with visual sort indicators (asc/desc arrows). 3. Apply design tokens for table header, row, and hover styles. 4. Handle empty state (no matches) with an appropriate EmptyState component or message. 5. Ensure the table is responsive (horizontal scroll on mobile). 6. Integrate MatchHistory DataTable into the /profile page layout below EvolutionChart.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render MatchHistory with real match data and verify sorting works for each column. Test with empty match list to confirm empty state. Validate keyboard navigation through table rows and sort controls.

subtasks.title: Implement PositionStats Visual Map and Spider Chart
subtasks.description: Build the PositionStats section featuring a visual position map and a spider/radar chart to display position-specific performance metrics.
subtasks.details: 1. Create a PositionStats component with two sub-sections: a visual field/position map and a spider (radar) chart. 2. For the position map, render a simplified football field SVG or image with the player's position highlighted using branded colors. 3. For the spider chart, use the charting library to render a radar chart with axes for key position metrics (e.g., pace, passing, shooting, defending, dribbling, physicality). 4. Map player position stats data to both visualizations. 5. Apply design tokens for colors, labels, and grid lines. 6. Ensure both visualizations are accessible and responsive. 7. Integrate PositionStats into the /profile page layout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render PositionStats with real position data and verify the position map highlights the correct position and the spider chart displays accurate metric values. Test with missing metrics to confirm graceful degradation.

subtasks.title: Build PendingInvitesCard and QuickClubRefreshCard with StatusBadge
subtasks.description: Implement PendingInvitesCard and QuickClubRefreshCard components, each using StatusBadge to reflect current invite/club status.
subtasks.details: 1. Create PendingInvitesCard component that lists pending team/club invites with accept/decline actions. 2. Create QuickClubRefreshCard component that displays current club info and a refresh/sync action. 3. Integrate StatusBadge into both cards to show status (e.g., pending, accepted, expired, synced, error) using design token colors. 4. Apply card layout and spacing from design-system tokens. 5. Handle empty states (no pending invites, no club linked). 6. Wire up action handlers (accept/decline invite, trigger club refresh) with loading and error feedback. 7. Integrate both cards into the /profile page sidebar or designated section.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render both cards with real invite and club data, verify StatusBadge reflects correct status, and test accept/decline/refresh actions. Test empty states and error states for each card.

subtasks.title: Add Suspense Boundaries and LoadingSkeleton per Profile Section
subtasks.description: Wrap each profile page section in React Suspense boundaries and implement section-specific LoadingSkeleton components for progressive loading.
subtasks.details: 1. Wrap PlayerHeader, StatsOverview, EvolutionChart, MatchHistory, PositionStats, PendingInvitesCard, and QuickClubRefreshCard each in their own React Suspense boundary. 2. Create or reuse LoadingSkeleton variants that match the shape and size of each section (e.g., avatar skeleton, stat card grid skeleton, chart skeleton, table row skeletons, card skeletons). 3. Ensure skeletons use animated shimmer effect via CSS or a utility class from the design system. 4. Add error boundaries per section to catch and display section-level errors without crashing the full page. 5. Validate that sections load independently and progressively as data resolves. 6. Follow React 18 Suspense patterns and ensure compatibility with the data-fetching strategy (e.g., React Query, SWR, or server components).
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate slow network or delayed data fetching and verify each section shows its LoadingSkeleton independently. Trigger errors per section and confirm error boundaries display fallback UI without affecting other sections.

subtasks.title: End-to-End Testing, All States Validation, and Lighthouse 90+ Performance Audit
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive tests covering real/empty data, all loading/error/empty states, DataTable sort/filter, chart interactions, and validate Lighthouse performance score of 90+.
subtasks.details: 1. Write integration/E2E tests (e.g., Playwright or Cypress) covering the full /profile page with real player data. 2. Test all loading states by mocking delayed API responses and verifying LoadingSkeleton renders. 3. Test empty states for each section (no matches, no stats, no invites, no club). 4. Test error states by mocking API failures and verifying error boundary fallback UI. 5. Test DataTable sorting for all columns in MatchHistory. 6. Test EvolutionChart and PositionStats spider chart interactions (hover tooltip, keyboard navigation). 7. Run Lighthouse audit on /profile page and address any issues to achieve 90+ score in Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, and SEO. 8. Optimize bundle size, image loading (lazy/priority), and code splitting as needed to meet the Lighthouse target.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full Playwright/Cypress test suite against a staging environment with seeded real and empty data. Run Lighthouse CI in the pipeline and fail the build if any category scores below 90. Review and fix accessibility violations flagged by axe-core.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break profile page redesign into component-focused subtasks: (1) PlayerHeader with larger avatar and position badge, (2) StatsOverview with StatCard v2 grid and trend indicators, (3) EvolutionChart with branded colors and tooltip, (4) MatchHistory DataTable with sorting, (5) PositionStats visual map/spider chart, (6) PendingInvitesCard and QuickClubRefreshCard with StatusBadge, (7) Suspense boundaries and LoadingSkeleton per section, (8) Testing with real/empty data and all loading/error states. Validate Lighthouse 90+ performance.

tasks.title: Profile Sub-Pages Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /profile/matches (DataTable filters) and /profile/settings (forms layout)
tasks.details: 1. /profile/matches: DataTable with filters (match type/result/date). 2. /profile/settings: PlayerIdentityForm + PositionSettingsForm in clean layout. 3. Use shared components + states. 4. Breadcrumb navigation. 5. Form validation + loading states.
tasks.testStrategy: Test DataTable filters work with real data, form submission/validation, empty states, keyboard navigation, consistency with main profile page

subtasks.title: Implement /profile/matches DataTable with Filters
subtasks.description: Build the /profile/matches page featuring a DataTable with filter controls for match type, result, and date range. Ensure sorting and filtering work correctly with real match data.
subtasks.details: 1. Create the /profile/matches route/page component. 2. Integrate the shared DataTable component (reused from Task 33's MatchHistory). 3. Add filter controls: match type (friendly/ranked/etc.), result (win/loss/draw), and date range picker. 4. Wire filter state to DataTable data source — apply filters client-side or via query params. 5. Display relevant columns: date, opponent, match type, result, score. 6. Ensure sorting on all columns is functional. 7. Follow design tokens and visual style consistent with Task 33's MatchHistory DataTable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each filter (type, result, date) individually and in combination with real data. Verify DataTable sorting works per column. Confirm URL/query state reflects active filters for shareability.

subtasks.title: Implement /profile/settings Form Layout with PlayerIdentityForm and PositionSettingsForm
subtasks.description: Build the /profile/settings page with a clean, organized layout containing PlayerIdentityForm and PositionSettingsForm sections, following the design system spec.
subtasks.details: 1. Create the /profile/settings route/page component. 2. Lay out two distinct form sections: PlayerIdentityForm (username, avatar, display name, etc.) and PositionSettingsForm (preferred positions, role, etc.). 3. Use shadcn Input/Label/Select components consistent with Task 32's auth form patterns. 4. Apply design tokens for spacing, typography, and card containers. 5. Implement controlled form state for each form independently. 6. Add submit handlers with optimistic UI or loading feedback. 7. Ensure visual hierarchy clearly separates the two forms (section headings, dividers, or cards).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test form rendering with existing player data pre-populated. Verify each form submits independently. Check layout responsiveness on mobile and desktop. Confirm design token usage matches the design system.

subtasks.title: Integrate Shared Components: Breadcrumb Navigation and Form Validation
subtasks.description: Add breadcrumb navigation to both sub-pages and implement consistent form validation across /profile/settings forms, using shared components and patterns.
subtasks.details: 1. Implement a shared Breadcrumb component (e.g., Home > Profile > Matches / Settings) and place it at the top of both /profile/matches and /profile/settings pages. 2. Ensure breadcrumb links are functional and styled with design tokens. 3. Add client-side form validation to PlayerIdentityForm and PositionSettingsForm: required fields, character limits, format checks (e.g., username pattern). 4. Display inline validation error messages using shared error/helper text components. 5. Prevent form submission when validation fails and show appropriate feedback. 6. Reuse any validation utilities or schemas (e.g., Zod) already established in the project.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test breadcrumb links navigate correctly from both sub-pages. Test form validation triggers on submit and on blur. Verify error messages are accessible (aria-describedby). Confirm breadcrumb is visually consistent across both pages.

subtasks.title: Implement Loading States, Empty States, and Error Boundaries
subtasks.description: Add loading skeletons, empty state components, and error boundaries to both /profile/matches and /profile/settings to handle all data and async states gracefully.
subtasks.details: 1. Wrap data-fetching sections in Suspense with LoadingSkeleton components matching the layout of the DataTable (matches) and form fields (settings). 2. Implement an EmptyState component for /profile/matches when no matches exist or filters return no results — include a helpful message and CTA (e.g., 'Play your first match'). 3. Add error boundary components to catch fetch failures and display user-friendly error messages with retry options. 4. Show loading indicators on form submit buttons in /profile/settings during async save operations. 5. Ensure skeleton dimensions match actual content to prevent layout shift. 6. Reuse LoadingSkeleton and EmptyState components from Task 33 where applicable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate loading state by throttling network. Test empty state renders when match list is empty and when filters return zero results. Test error boundary by mocking a failed API call. Verify loading spinner appears on settings form submit.

subtasks.title: Keyboard Navigation, Accessibility, and Consistency Testing with Main Profile Page
subtasks.description: Audit and ensure full keyboard navigability, WCAG AA accessibility compliance, and visual/behavioral consistency between the sub-pages and the main /profile page (Task 33).
subtasks.details: 1. Verify full keyboard navigation on /profile/matches: tab through filter controls, DataTable rows, and pagination. 2. Verify full keyboard navigation on /profile/settings: tab through all form fields, submit buttons, and breadcrumb links. 3. Ensure focus indicators are visible and consistent with design tokens (matching Task 32's WCAG AA focus styles). 4. Cross-check visual design (typography, spacing, colors, component variants) against the main /profile page to ensure consistency. 5. Test with screen reader (e.g., VoiceOver or NVDA) for proper label announcements on filters and form fields. 6. Run Lighthouse accessibility audit and address any issues. 7. Confirm shared components (DataTable, StatCard, Breadcrumb) behave identically across /profile and its sub-pages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually tab through all interactive elements on both pages. Use axe DevTools or Lighthouse to audit accessibility score. Compare screenshots of sub-pages against /profile page for visual consistency. Test with at least one screen reader for label and error announcement correctness.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Organize profile sub-pages redesign into subtasks: (1) /profile/matches DataTable with filters (match type/result/date), (2) /profile/settings form layout with PlayerIdentityForm and PositionSettingsForm, (3) Shared component integration (breadcrumb, form validation), (4) Loading and empty states, (5) Keyboard navigation and consistency testing with main profile page.

tasks.title: Team Page Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /team with 4 tabs: Roster (DataTable), History (DataTable), Matchmaking (EmptyState), Lineup (visual field)
tasks.details: 1. Tabs: underline style (not pill). 2. Roster tab: TeamDashboard (StatCard grid), RosterTable (DataTable + PlayerAvatar). 3. History: MatchHistoryTable + ScoreDisplay. 4. Matchmaking: EmptyState → /matchmaking. 5. Lineup: LineupVisual polished field. 6. Invite Dialog (shadcn). 7. All states implemented.
tasks.testStrategy: Test tab switching, DataTable functionality, LineupVisual interactions, Dialog modal keyboard escape, empty/full data states, visual consistency

subtasks.title: Tab Navigation Setup with Underline Style
subtasks.description: Implement the 4-tab navigation structure for the /team page using underline style (not pill) tabs, including Roster, History, Matchmaking, and Lineup tabs.
subtasks.details: Use shadcn Tabs component configured with underline variant styling. Define tab triggers for Roster, History, Matchmaking, and Lineup. Apply design tokens for active/inactive tab states (border-bottom indicator, color transitions). Ensure tab labels are accessible with proper aria attributes. Set up tab content panels with lazy rendering to avoid unnecessary data fetching. Wire up URL-based or state-based tab persistence so refreshing or navigating back restores the active tab. Match the underline style from the design system spec (not pill/rounded).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test tab switching renders correct panel, active tab has underline indicator, keyboard navigation (arrow keys) cycles through tabs, URL/state reflects active tab, no visual regression on pill style.

subtasks.title: Roster Tab – TeamDashboard StatCard Grid
subtasks.description: Build the TeamDashboard section within the Roster tab, displaying a responsive StatCard grid with key team statistics.
subtasks.details: Create a TeamDashboard component that fetches team-level stats (wins, losses, goals scored, goals conceded, win rate, etc.) and renders them in a StatCard v2 grid layout. Use the shared StatCard component with trend indicators. Apply responsive grid (2-col mobile, 4-col desktop). Handle loading skeleton state per card and empty/error states gracefully. Integrate with the team data API/store. Ensure design tokens (colors, spacing, typography) are consistent with the design system.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test StatCard grid renders with real team data, loading skeletons appear during fetch, empty state shown when no stats, responsive layout at mobile/desktop breakpoints, visual consistency with StatCard v2 spec.

subtasks.title: Roster Tab – RosterTable with DataTable and PlayerAvatar
subtasks.description: Implement the RosterTable component within the Roster tab using a DataTable with PlayerAvatar, player name, position, and stats columns.
subtasks.details: Build RosterTable using the shared DataTable component. Columns: PlayerAvatar + player name (linked to /profile), PositionBadge, key stats (matches played, goals, assists, rating). Support sorting on all numeric columns. Add PlayerAvatar component rendering player photo or initials fallback. Implement loading skeleton rows and empty state (no players on roster). Fetch roster data from team API. Ensure DataTable pagination or scroll handles large rosters. Apply consistent row hover states and spacing from design tokens.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test DataTable renders roster rows, sorting works on numeric columns, PlayerAvatar shows image or initials fallback, empty state shown when roster is empty, loading skeleton appears during fetch, links to player profiles work.

subtasks.title: History Tab – MatchHistoryTable with ScoreDisplay
subtasks.description: Build the History tab with a MatchHistoryTable DataTable component and ScoreDisplay for each match result.
subtasks.details: Create MatchHistoryTable using the shared DataTable component. Columns: date, opponent (with team avatar), ScoreDisplay (home-away score with win/loss/draw color coding), match type, and a details link. Implement ScoreDisplay as a sub-component showing score in a styled badge (green=win, red=loss, grey=draw). Support sorting by date and filtering by result type. Handle loading skeleton, empty state (no matches played yet), and error state. Fetch match history from the team history API endpoint. Ensure pagination for long histories.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test MatchHistoryTable renders with real match data, ScoreDisplay shows correct colors for win/loss/draw, sorting by date works, empty state shown when no history, loading skeleton appears, pagination navigates correctly.

subtasks.title: Matchmaking Tab – EmptyState with Navigation to /matchmaking
subtasks.description: Implement the Matchmaking tab displaying an EmptyState component with a call-to-action button that navigates to /matchmaking.
subtasks.details: Render the shared EmptyState component inside the Matchmaking tab panel. Configure EmptyState with an appropriate icon (e.g., trophy or search), heading ('No active matchmaking'), descriptive subtext, and a primary CTA button 'Find a Match' that navigates to /matchmaking using Next.js router. Ensure the EmptyState is centered vertically within the tab panel. Apply design tokens for spacing and typography. This tab intentionally has no data-fetching logic — it is a static redirect prompt.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test EmptyState renders with correct icon, heading, and subtext, CTA button navigates to /matchmaking, component is visually centered, no data fetch is triggered on tab activation.

subtasks.title: Lineup Tab – LineupVisual Polished Field Component
subtasks.description: Build the Lineup tab with a LineupVisual component rendering a polished soccer field with player positions overlaid.
subtasks.details: Create LineupVisual as a styled SVG or CSS-based soccer field with formation zones. Fetch the team's current lineup/formation from the API. Render PlayerAvatar + name at each position on the field. Support common formations (4-4-2, 4-3-3, etc.) with dynamic positioning. Add hover tooltips showing player stats. Handle empty lineup state (show field with empty position slots). Ensure the field is responsive and scales correctly on mobile. Apply design tokens for field colors, player markers, and typography. Polish with subtle field texture/lines.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test LineupVisual renders correct formation with player avatars, hover tooltips show player info, empty slots shown when lineup is incomplete, field scales responsively, formation changes re-render positions correctly.

subtasks.title: Invite Dialog Implementation via shadcn
subtasks.description: Implement the Invite Player dialog using shadcn Dialog component, allowing team managers to invite players by username or email.
subtasks.details: Create an InviteDialog component using shadcn Dialog (modal). Trigger button placed in the Roster tab header area. Dialog contains: title 'Invite Player', a search/input field for username or email, a submit button, and a cancel button. Implement form validation (required field, valid email format if email). On submit, call the invite API endpoint and show success/error toast feedback. Handle loading state on submit button. Ensure keyboard accessibility: Escape closes dialog, focus trap inside modal, focus returns to trigger on close. Apply design tokens for dialog styling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test dialog opens on trigger click, Escape key closes dialog, form validation shows errors on empty/invalid input, successful invite shows success toast, API error shows error toast, focus trap works inside modal, loading state disables submit button.

subtasks.title: State Management, Data Loading, and Visual Consistency Testing
subtasks.description: Implement unified state management for all tabs, ensure data loading strategies are correct, and perform end-to-end visual consistency and interaction testing across the entire Team page.
subtasks.details: Set up a centralized data-fetching strategy (React Query or SWR) for team page data: roster, match history, and lineup. Ensure tab switching does not re-fetch already-loaded data (cache). Implement global loading skeleton for initial page load and per-tab loading states. Add error boundaries per tab section. Verify all empty states, loading states, and error states are implemented consistently across all 4 tabs. Run visual consistency audit: spacing, typography, color tokens, component variants match design system. Ensure Lighthouse performance score is acceptable. Fix any cross-tab state leakage or stale data issues.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test tab switching preserves loaded data (no redundant fetches), all loading/empty/error states render correctly per tab, Invite Dialog state resets on close, keyboard navigation works across all tabs and interactive elements, visual regression check against design spec, Lighthouse audit passes performance thresholds.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Structure team page redesign into tab-focused subtasks: (1) Tab navigation setup (underline style), (2) Roster tab with TeamDashboard StatCard grid and RosterTable DataTable, (3) History tab with MatchHistoryTable and ScoreDisplay, (4) Matchmaking tab with EmptyState and navigation, (5) Lineup tab with LineupVisual field, (6) Invite Dialog implementation via shadcn, (7) State management for all tabs and data loading, (8) Testing tab switching, DataTable functionality, and visual consistency.

tasks.title: Team Sub-Pages Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /team/claim wizard (3-step progress indicator), /team/lineup drag-drop field, and other team sub-pages. Apply design tokens and shared components.

subtasks.title: Implement /team/claim Wizard with 3-Step Progress Indicator
subtasks.description: Build the /team/claim multi-step wizard UI featuring a 3-step progress indicator, step navigation (back/next/submit), and per-step form validation using design tokens and shared components.
subtasks.details: 1. Create a WizardLayout component with a 3-step progress bar/stepper using design tokens (colors, spacing, typography). 2. Define the three steps: e.g., (1) Team Search/Selection, (2) Role & Position Confirmation, (3) Review & Submit. 3. Implement step-level form validation using react-hook-form or zod schemas — prevent advancing until current step is valid. 4. Add Back/Next/Submit navigation buttons with loading and disabled states. 5. Show inline error messages per field. 6. Integrate breadcrumb navigation consistent with other sub-pages. 7. Reuse shared Input, Label, Button, and StatusBadge components from the design system. 8. Handle success redirect and error boundary states after submission.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each wizard step renders correctly, form validation blocks progression on invalid input, back navigation preserves entered data, submission triggers correct API call, loading/error/success states display properly, and keyboard navigation works across all steps.

subtasks.title: Implement /team/lineup Drag-and-Drop Field
subtasks.description: Build the /team/lineup page with an interactive drag-and-drop soccer field where players can be repositioned across formation slots, with full accessibility and touch support.
subtasks.details: 1. Use a drag-and-drop library (e.g., @dnd-kit/core or react-beautiful-dnd) to enable dragging player cards onto field position slots. 2. Render a visual soccer field (LineupVisual component from Task 35) as the drop target with clearly defined position zones. 3. Support swapping players between positions by dropping one card onto an occupied slot. 4. Implement keyboard accessibility: arrow keys to move focus between slots, Enter/Space to pick up and place players. 5. Add touch/pointer event support for mobile. 6. Show player name, avatar (PlayerAvatar), and position badge on each draggable card. 7. Persist lineup changes via API call on save. 8. Display loading skeleton while fetching current lineup data. 9. Apply design tokens for field colors, card styles, and drag-over highlight states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test drag-and-drop reordering works with mouse and touch, keyboard accessibility allows full lineup editing without a mouse, swapping occupied slots behaves correctly, save action persists changes, loading/empty states render properly, and WCAG AA focus indicators are visible.

subtasks.title: Identify and Specify Remaining Team Sub-Pages
subtasks.description: Audit all team sub-pages beyond /team/claim and /team/lineup, document their current state, and produce design/implementation specifications for each remaining page.
subtasks.details: 1. Enumerate all routes under /team/* (e.g., /team/settings, /team/invites, /team/history detail views, etc.) by reviewing the router config and existing page files. 2. For each identified sub-page, document: current UI state, required data, user actions, and edge cases (empty, loading, error). 3. Map which shared components (DataTable, EmptyState, StatCard, StatusBadge, etc.) apply to each page. 4. Produce a specification document or inline code comments describing the redesign for each page. 5. Prioritize pages by user impact and dependency on Task 35 components. 6. Flag any pages that require new shared components not yet built.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review specification completeness with a checklist: each sub-page has defined states (loading/empty/error/populated), component mapping, and acceptance criteria. Validate route coverage against the router config file.

subtasks.title: Apply Design Tokens and Shared Components Across All Team Sub-Pages
subtasks.description: Systematically apply the project's design tokens (colors, spacing, typography, shadows) and shared components to all team sub-pages, ensuring visual consistency with the rest of the redesigned app.
subtasks.details: 1. Audit all team sub-page files for hardcoded color, spacing, or font values and replace with design token CSS variables or Tailwind token classes. 2. Replace any one-off UI elements with canonical shared components: Button, Input, Label, PlayerAvatar, PositionBadge, StatusBadge, DataTable, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, Breadcrumb. 3. Ensure consistent page-level layout: breadcrumb at top, section headings, responsive grid/stack. 4. Apply Suspense + LoadingSkeleton per section following the pattern established in Tasks 33–35. 5. Verify dark/light mode token usage is consistent. 6. Run a visual diff or Storybook review against the design spec to catch inconsistencies. 7. Ensure all interactive elements meet WCAG AA contrast ratios using design tokens.
subtasks.testStrategy: Visual regression test (e.g., Chromatic or Percy) comparing before/after screenshots. Manually verify token usage with browser DevTools. Run axe or Lighthouse accessibility audit to confirm WCAG AA compliance. Cross-check against design spec for each sub-page.

subtasks.title: End-to-End Testing: Wizard Flow, Drag-Drop Interactions, and Form Validation
subtasks.description: Write and execute comprehensive tests covering the /team/claim wizard flow, /team/lineup drag-and-drop interactions, and form validation across all team sub-pages.
subtasks.details: 1. Wizard flow tests (e2e with Playwright or Cypress): complete happy path through all 3 steps, validation blocking on each step, back-navigation data persistence, submission success/error handling. 2. Drag-and-drop tests: drag player to empty slot, swap two players, keyboard-only lineup editing, touch simulation on mobile viewport. 3. Form validation unit tests (Vitest + React Testing Library): required field errors, invalid format errors, async validation (e.g., team name already taken). 4. Integration tests for API interactions: mock API responses for claim submission and lineup save, assert correct payloads. 5. Accessibility tests: run axe-core on wizard and lineup pages, assert no critical violations. 6. Regression tests for all other team sub-pages: loading, empty, and error states render without console errors.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI pipeline runs unit + integration tests on every PR. E2E tests run against a staging environment with seeded data. Accessibility audit runs as part of CI using axe-playwright. Test coverage report must show >80% coverage for new wizard and drag-drop components.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose team sub-pages into focused subtasks: (1) /team/claim wizard with 3-step progress indicator and form validation, (2) /team/lineup with drag-drop field implementation, (3) Other team sub-pages identification and specification, (4) Design token and shared component application, (5) Testing wizard flow, drag-drop interactions, and form validation.

tasks.title: Matchmaking Page Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /matchmaking with animated QueuePanel, styled ConfrontationChat with message bubbles, visual countdown, and status indicators (searching, matched, confirmed, expired).

subtasks.title: QueuePanel Component with Animated State Transitions
subtasks.description: Build the QueuePanel component for the /matchmaking page with smooth animated transitions between queue states (idle, searching, matched, confirmed, expired).
subtasks.details: Create a QueuePanel React component that visually represents the user's current queue status. Implement CSS/Framer Motion animations for state transitions (e.g., pulsing animation while searching, pop-in animation on match found). Use a state machine pattern (e.g., XState or local useReducer) to manage transitions between idle → searching → matched → confirmed/expired. Ensure animations are performant (use transform/opacity, avoid layout thrashing). Apply design system tokens for colors, spacing, and typography consistent with the project's design language. Include a cancel queue button with confirmation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each state transition visually in Storybook. Verify animations run at 60fps using browser DevTools. Unit test state machine transitions to ensure invalid transitions are blocked. Check accessibility (ARIA live regions for status changes).

subtasks.title: ConfrontationChat Component with Message Bubbles and Styling
subtasks.description: Design and implement the ConfrontationChat component with styled message bubbles, timestamps, sender differentiation, and a message input area for matched players to communicate.
subtasks.details: Build a ConfrontationChat React component that renders a scrollable message list with distinct bubble styles for the current user (right-aligned, primary color) vs. opponent (left-aligned, neutral color). Include sender avatar/name, timestamp formatting, and auto-scroll to latest message. Add a text input with send button (Enter key support). Apply shadcn UI components where applicable. Handle empty state (no messages yet) and loading state (skeleton bubbles). Ensure the chat panel is only visible/active when status is 'matched' or 'confirmed'. Style consistently with the project's design system.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test message rendering with mock data for both sender types. Verify auto-scroll behavior on new messages. Test keyboard interaction (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline). Check empty and loading states render correctly. Visual regression test for bubble styling.

subtasks.title: Visual Countdown Timer Implementation
subtasks.description: Implement a visually engaging countdown timer component that displays remaining time for match confirmation, with animated progress and urgency indicators as time runs low.
subtasks.details: Create a CountdownTimer React component using useEffect and useRef for precise interval management (avoid drift). Display time in MM:SS format with a circular or linear animated progress bar (SVG or CSS). Implement color transitions as time decreases (e.g., green → yellow → red at thresholds like 50% and 20% remaining). Add a pulsing/shake animation in the final 10 seconds for urgency. Accept props: totalSeconds, onExpire callback. Ensure the timer pauses/resets correctly on state changes (e.g., if match is confirmed before expiry). Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth animation updates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test timer accuracy over a 60-second interval (allow <100ms drift). Test onExpire callback fires correctly. Test color threshold transitions at correct percentages. Verify timer resets properly when component unmounts or state changes. Test urgency animation triggers at correct threshold.

subtasks.title: Status Indicators with Visual Feedback (Searching, Matched, Confirmed, Expired)
subtasks.description: Implement a comprehensive status indicator system that provides clear visual feedback for all four matchmaking states: searching, matched, confirmed, and expired.
subtasks.details: Create a StatusIndicator component that renders distinct visual treatments for each state: (1) Searching — animated spinner/pulse with 'Finding opponent...' text and elapsed time; (2) Matched — success icon with green highlight and opponent info reveal animation; (3) Confirmed — checkmark animation with both players confirmed badges; (4) Expired — warning/timeout icon with red tint and retry CTA. Use Framer Motion or CSS keyframes for entrance/exit animations between states. Include ARIA live regions (aria-live='polite') for screen reader announcements on state changes. Apply design system color tokens (success, warning, error, info) for semantic color coding.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each status renders correct icon, color, and text. Verify ARIA live region announces state changes. Test transition animations between all state combinations. Check that the expired state shows a functional retry button. Accessibility audit with screen reader simulation.

subtasks.title: Real-Time State Management and WebSocket Integration
subtasks.description: Implement real-time state management for the matchmaking page using WebSocket (or Supabase Realtime) to synchronize queue status, match events, and chat messages between players.
subtasks.details: Set up a WebSocket connection (or Supabase Realtime channel) for the matchmaking session. Implement a global/context-level state manager (Zustand or React Context + useReducer) to hold: currentStatus, matchData (opponent info, match ID), messages[], countdown start time, and confirmations. Handle WebSocket events: 'queue_joined', 'match_found', 'player_confirmed', 'match_expired', 'chat_message'. Implement reconnection logic with exponential backoff. Emit events: join_queue, confirm_match, send_message, leave_queue. Ensure optimistic UI updates for chat messages. Clean up WebSocket connection on component unmount. Sync countdown timer start time from server to avoid client drift.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test WebSocket event handling with a mock WebSocket server. Test reconnection logic triggers after connection drop. Verify state updates correctly for each event type. Test optimistic message updates and rollback on failure. Test cleanup on unmount prevents memory leaks.

subtasks.title: Testing Animations, State Transitions, and User Interactions
subtasks.description: Comprehensive testing of the complete matchmaking page redesign covering animation performance, all state transition flows, user interactions, accessibility, and mobile responsiveness.
subtasks.details: Write end-to-end tests (Playwright or Cypress) covering the full matchmaking flow: enter queue → match found → chat → confirm → success. Test all edge cases: match expiry, opponent disconnect, network interruption, re-queue after expiry. Performance audit: measure animation FPS, check for layout thrashing, verify no jank during state transitions using Chrome DevTools Performance tab. Accessibility audit: keyboard navigation through all interactive elements, screen reader flow, color contrast ratios (WCAG AA). Mobile responsiveness: test on 375px, 768px, 1280px viewports. Visual regression tests with Percy or Chromatic for all 4 status states. Test concurrent actions (e.g., chat message arriving during countdown).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Playwright E2E suite against staging environment with mock WebSocket server. Use Lighthouse for performance scoring (target >90). Run axe-core accessibility scan on each status state. Compare visual snapshots against approved baselines. Test on real mobile device or BrowserStack for touch interactions.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Structure matchmaking page redesign into subtasks: (1) QueuePanel with animated state transitions, (2) ConfrontationChat with message bubbles and styling, (3) Visual countdown timer implementation, (4) Status indicators (searching, matched, confirmed, expired) with visual feedback, (5) Real-time state management and WebSocket integration, (6) Testing animations, state transitions, and user interactions.

tasks.title: Tournament Pages Redesign
tasks.description: Redesign /tournaments list with banner-style TournamentCard (VirtualNexusArena inspiration), and /tournaments/[id] detail page migrating inline styles to Tailwind tokens. Refine BracketView, HallOfFameCard, EnrollButton.

subtasks.title: Redesign /tournaments List Page with Banner-Style Layout
subtasks.description: Rebuild the /tournaments list page to feature a banner-style layout inspired by VirtualNexusArena, integrating the new TournamentCard component in a responsive grid/list structure with proper filtering, sorting, and empty/loading states.
subtasks.details: 1. Scaffold the /tournaments page with a hero/banner section at the top (featured tournament or promotional banner). 2. Implement a responsive grid layout (e.g., 1-col mobile, 2-col tablet, 3-col desktop) using Tailwind tokens. 3. Add filter controls (status: upcoming/ongoing/completed, game type) and a search bar. 4. Integrate LoadingSkeleton placeholders while data fetches. 5. Implement EmptyState component when no tournaments match filters. 6. Use Suspense boundaries for async tournament data. 7. Ensure breadcrumb navigation and page title are consistent with design system. 8. Apply VirtualNexusArena-inspired visual language: dark backgrounds, neon accent borders, bold typography.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test page renders with real tournament data and empty data, verify filter/search interactions update the list, check loading skeletons appear during fetch, validate responsive layout at mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints, confirm EmptyState displays correctly.

subtasks.title: Build TournamentCard Component with Image, Title, Status, and CTA
subtasks.description: Create a reusable banner-style TournamentCard component displaying tournament image/banner, title, game type, status badge, date/time info, and a prominent Call-To-Action button, following the VirtualNexusArena design inspiration.
subtasks.details: 1. Create `components/tournaments/TournamentCard.tsx` with props: id, title, bannerImage, status, gameType, startDate, participantCount, maxParticipants, ctaLabel. 2. Implement banner image with Next.js Image component (aspect ratio 16:9 or 21:9). 3. Add StatusBadge (upcoming/ongoing/completed) with color-coded Tailwind tokens. 4. Display title in bold, large typography with text truncation for overflow. 5. Show participant count progress bar (enrolled/max). 6. CTA button links to /tournaments/[id] or triggers EnrollButton flow. 7. Add hover effects: scale transform, border glow using Tailwind transitions. 8. Ensure full keyboard accessibility (focus ring, aria-labels). 9. Support skeleton loading variant via a TournamentCardSkeleton sub-component.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render TournamentCard with all prop variants (upcoming, ongoing, completed), verify image loads with fallback, test CTA button navigation, check hover/focus states visually, validate accessibility with keyboard navigation and screen reader labels, test skeleton variant renders correctly.

subtasks.title: Redesign /tournaments/[id] Detail Page with Tailwind Token Migration
subtasks.description: Rebuild the tournament detail page /tournaments/[id], systematically migrating all inline styles to Tailwind utility classes and design tokens, while restructuring the layout to include tournament metadata, bracket section, hall of fame, and enrollment area.
subtasks.details: 1. Audit existing /tournaments/[id] page for all inline `style={{}}` props and document them. 2. Map each inline style value to the closest Tailwind token (colors → design system palette, spacing → Tailwind scale, typography → font-size/weight tokens). 3. Restructure page layout: hero banner (reuse TournamentCard banner image), metadata section (dates, format, prize pool, rules), BracketView section, HallOfFameCard section, EnrollButton sticky footer or sidebar. 4. Add Suspense + LoadingSkeleton for each major section. 5. Implement error boundary for failed tournament data fetch. 6. Add breadcrumb: Home > Tournaments > [Tournament Name]. 7. Ensure all section headings use consistent typography tokens. 8. Remove all inline styles — zero tolerance policy enforced via ESLint rule if available.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify zero inline styles remain using ESLint or manual audit, test page renders with real tournament data, check all sections display correctly, validate loading/error states, test breadcrumb navigation, confirm responsive layout across breakpoints.

subtasks.title: Refine BracketView Component with Interactions and Visual Polish
subtasks.description: Enhance the BracketView component used on the tournament detail page with improved visual design, interactive match node highlighting, zoom/pan support for large brackets, and responsive behavior for mobile viewing.
subtasks.details: 1. Audit existing BracketView for layout issues, hardcoded colors, and missing interactions. 2. Migrate all inline styles to Tailwind tokens (border colors, backgrounds, text). 3. Implement match node hover state: highlight connected matches and participants. 4. Add click interaction on match nodes to show a popover/tooltip with match details (teams, score, date). 5. For large brackets (8+ teams), implement horizontal scroll with optional zoom controls (zoom in/out buttons). 6. Add winner path highlighting: visually trace the winning team's path through the bracket. 7. Implement responsive collapse: on mobile, show a simplified list view of matches instead of the full bracket tree. 8. Add loading skeleton for bracket data. 9. Use CSS transitions for smooth hover/highlight animations. 10. Ensure bracket connectors (lines between nodes) render correctly at all viewport sizes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test BracketView with small (4-team) and large (16-team) bracket data, verify hover highlighting works on match nodes, test popover displays correct match info, validate zoom/pan on large brackets, check mobile list view renders correctly, confirm loading skeleton appears during data fetch.

subtasks.title: Build HallOfFameCard and EnrollButton Components
subtasks.description: Create the HallOfFameCard component to showcase tournament winners/top performers, and refine the EnrollButton component with proper enrollment states (enrolled, pending, full, closed) and confirmation flow.
subtasks.details: 1. HallOfFameCard: Create `components/tournaments/HallOfFameCard.tsx` displaying winner avatar (PlayerAvatar), player name, team name, achievement label (e.g., 'Champion', 'Runner-Up', 'MVP'), and tournament edition. Support a ranked list variant for top-3 podium display. Apply gold/silver/bronze accent colors via Tailwind tokens. Add subtle shimmer/glow effect on the champion card. 2. EnrollButton: Refine `components/tournaments/EnrollButton.tsx` with states: idle (Enroll Now), loading (spinner), enrolled (checkmark + Enrolled), full (disabled + Tournament Full), closed (disabled + Enrollment Closed). Implement confirmation dialog (shadcn Dialog) before submitting enrollment. Add optimistic UI update on enrollment. Handle error state with toast notification. Ensure button is fully accessible with aria-disabled, aria-label, and keyboard support. 3. Both components must use zero inline styles — Tailwind tokens only.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test HallOfFameCard renders with champion, runner-up, and MVP data; verify podium styling for top-3. Test EnrollButton in all states (idle, loading, enrolled, full, closed), verify confirmation dialog appears before enrollment, test optimistic UI update, check error toast on failed enrollment, validate keyboard accessibility for both components.

subtasks.title: Testing: Card Rendering, Bracket Interactions, and Responsive Design
subtasks.description: Conduct comprehensive testing across all tournament page components covering TournamentCard rendering, BracketView interactions, HallOfFameCard and EnrollButton behavior, and responsive design validation across all breakpoints.
subtasks.details: 1. Write unit tests for TournamentCard with all status variants (upcoming, ongoing, completed) and edge cases (missing image, long title truncation, full participant count). 2. Write unit tests for HallOfFameCard with champion/runner-up/MVP data and empty hall of fame state. 3. Write unit tests for EnrollButton covering all states and the confirmation dialog flow. 4. Write integration tests for /tournaments list page: filter interactions, search, empty state, loading state. 5. Write integration tests for /tournaments/[id] detail page: data loading, error boundary, breadcrumb navigation. 6. Test BracketView interactions: hover highlighting, popover display, zoom controls, mobile list view. 7. Perform responsive design testing at breakpoints: 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop), 1920px (wide). 8. Run Lighthouse audit on both /tournaments and /tournaments/[id] targeting 90+ performance score. 9. Validate WCAG AA accessibility: keyboard navigation, focus management, color contrast ratios. 10. Confirm zero inline styles across all new/modified components.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute full test suite with Jest + React Testing Library for unit/integration tests, use Playwright or Cypress for E2E bracket interaction tests, run Lighthouse CI for performance scores, use axe-core for automated accessibility checks, manually verify responsive layouts in browser DevTools at all target breakpoints.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break tournament pages redesign into subtasks: (1) /tournaments list page with banner-style TournamentCard (VirtualNexusArena inspiration), (2) TournamentCard component with image, title, status, and CTA, (3) /tournaments/[id] detail page with inline style migration to Tailwind tokens, (4) BracketView refinement and interaction, (5) HallOfFameCard and EnrollButton components, (6) Testing card rendering, bracket interactions, and responsive design.

tasks.title: Detail Pages Redesign - Players and Matches
tasks.description: Redesign /players/[id] with player profile card, StatCard v2 grid, DataTable history. Redesign /matches/[id] with ScoreDisplay, home/away DataTables. Add Breadcrumb navigation to both.

subtasks.title: Redesign /players/[id] - Player Profile Card Layout
subtasks.description: Build the player profile card layout for the /players/[id] detail page, including avatar, name, position badge, and key identity fields.
subtasks.details: Implement the top-level player profile card using shared components: PlayerAvatar (large variant), PositionBadge, and player identity fields (name, team, joined date). Ensure dynamic routing via Next.js [id] param with proper data fetching (SSR or client-side with loading/error states). Apply design system tokens for spacing, typography, and color. Handle edge cases: player not found (404 state), loading skeleton, and error boundary. Reference Task 33's PlayerHeader implementation for consistency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with valid player ID, invalid/nonexistent ID (404), and slow network (loading skeleton). Verify PlayerAvatar and PositionBadge render correctly with real data. Check mobile responsiveness and visual consistency with other detail pages.

subtasks.title: Redesign /players/[id] - StatCard v2 Grid for Player Statistics
subtasks.description: Implement a StatCard v2 grid section on the /players/[id] page to display key player statistics such as goals, assists, matches played, and win rate.
subtasks.details: Below the profile card, render a responsive StatCard v2 grid (2-col mobile, 4-col desktop) using the shared StatCard v2 component. Stats to display: goals, assists, matches played, win rate, and any position-specific metrics. Fetch player stats from the appropriate API/Supabase query tied to the [id] param. Apply trend indicators where applicable (up/down arrows). Use LoadingSkeleton per card during data fetch. Align with StatCard v2 usage in Task 33's StatsOverview for visual consistency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test grid renders with full data, partial data, and empty/zero stats. Verify responsive layout at mobile and desktop breakpoints. Check trend indicators display correctly. Confirm loading skeletons appear during fetch.

subtasks.title: Redesign /players/[id] - DataTable for Match History
subtasks.description: Add a DataTable component to the /players/[id] page displaying the player's match history with sorting and relevant columns.
subtasks.details: Implement a DataTable below the StatCard grid showing the player's match history. Columns: date, opponent, result (win/loss/draw), score, player rating or contribution. Support column sorting (date, result). Fetch match history filtered by player [id]. Handle empty state (no matches played yet) with an EmptyState component. Apply pagination or infinite scroll if the dataset is large. Reuse the DataTable component consistent with Task 34's /profile/matches implementation. Include loading skeleton for the table body.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test DataTable with players having many matches, one match, and zero matches (empty state). Verify sorting works on all sortable columns. Check pagination/scroll behavior. Confirm data is correctly filtered to the specific player ID.

subtasks.title: Redesign /matches/[id] - ScoreDisplay Component Integration
subtasks.description: Build the /matches/[id] detail page header using the ScoreDisplay component to show match result, teams, score, date, and match metadata.
subtasks.details: Implement the /matches/[id] page with ScoreDisplay as the hero section. ScoreDisplay should show: home team name/logo, away team name/logo, final score (or 'upcoming' state if not played), match date/time, and match type/competition label. Fetch match data dynamically via the [id] param with SSR or client-side fetching. Handle states: loading skeleton, match not found (404), upcoming match (no score yet), and completed match.
